1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module HasUrl
     4	  extend ActiveSupport::Concern
     5	
     6	  class_methods do
     7	    def extract_url(url)
     8	      url.match(self::URL_REGEX)
     9	    end
    10	
    11	    def extract_sha1(path)
    12	      data = extract_url(path)
    13	      return if data.blank?
    14	
    15	      sha1 = data[2]
    16	      return if sha1&.length != Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
    17	
    18	      sha1
    19	    end
    20	
    21	    def get_from_url(url)
    22	      return if url.blank?
    23	
    24	      uri =
    25	        begin
    26	          URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
    27	        rescue URI::Error
    28	        end
    29	
    30	      return if uri&.path.blank?
    31	      data = extract_url(uri.path)
    32	
    33	      if data.blank?
    34	        result = nil
    35	        result ||= self.find_by(url: uri.path)
    36	        return result
    37	      end
    38	
    39	      result = nil
    40	
    41	      if self.name == "Upload"
    42	        sha1 = data[2]
    43	        result = self.find_by(sha1: sha1) if sha1&.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
    44	      end
    45	
    46	      result || self.find_by("url LIKE ?", "%#{data[1]}")
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    def get_from_urls(upload_urls)
    50	      urls = []
    51	      sha1s = []
    52	
    53	      upload_urls.each do |url|
    54	        next if url.blank?
    55	
    56	        uri =
    57	          begin
    58	            URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
    59	          rescue URI::Error
    60	          end
    61	
    62	        next if uri&.path.blank?
    63	        urls << uri.path
    64	
    65	        if data = extract_url(uri.path).presence
    66	          urls << data[1]
    67	          sha1s << data[2] if self.name == "Upload"
    68	        end
    69	      end
    70	
    71	      self.where(url: urls).or(self.where(sha1: sha1s))
    72	    end
    73	  end
    74	end

     3	class UploadReference < ActiveRecord::Base
     4	  belongs_to :upload
     5	  belongs_to :target, polymorphic: true
     6	
     7	  delegate :to_markdown, to: :upload
     8	
     9	  def self.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [], target: nil, target_type: nil, target_id: nil)
    10	    if !target && !(target_type && target_id)
    11	      raise "target OR target_type and target_id are required"
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    if target.present?
    15	      target_type = target.class
    16	      target_id = target.id
    17	    end
    18	
    19	    upload_ids = upload_ids.uniq.reject(&:blank?)
    20	    target_type = target_type.to_s
    21	
    22	    if upload_ids.empty?
    23	      UploadReference.where(target_type: target_type, target_id: target_id).delete_all
    24	
    25	      return
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    rows =
    29	      upload_ids.map do |upload_id|
    30	        {
    31	          upload_id: upload_id,
    32	          target_type: target_type,
    33	          target_id: target_id,
    34	          created_at: Time.zone.now,
    35	          updated_at: Time.zone.now,
    36	        }
    37	      end
    38	
    39	    UploadReference.transaction do |transaction|
    40	      UploadReference
    41	        .where(target_type: target_type, target_id: target_id)
    42	        .where.not(upload_id: upload_ids)
    43	        .delete_all
    44	
    45	      UploadReference.insert_all(rows)
    46	    end
    47	  end
    48	end

     3	class OptimizedImage < ActiveRecord::Base
     4	  include HasUrl
     5	  belongs_to :upload
     6	
     7	  # BUMP UP if optimized image algorithm changes
     8	  VERSION = 2
     9	  URL_REGEX = %r{(/optimized/\dX[/\.\w]*/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)[\.\w]*)}
    10	
    11	  def self.lock(upload_id, width, height)
    12	    @hostname ||= Discourse.os_hostname
    13	    # note, the extra lock here ensures we only optimize one image per machine on webs
    14	    # this can very easily lead to runaway CPU so slowing it down is beneficial and it is hijacked
    15	    #
    16	    # we can not afford this blocking in Sidekiq cause it can lead to starvation
    17	    if lock_per_machine?
    18	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_host_#{@hostname}") do
    19	        DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
    20	      end
    21	    else
    22	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
    23	    end
    24	  end
    25	
    26	  def self.lock_per_machine?
    27	    return @lock_per_machine if defined?(@lock_per_machine)
    28	    @lock_per_machine = !Sidekiq.server?
    29	  end
    30	
    31	  def self.lock_per_machine=(value)
    32	    @lock_per_machine = value
    33	  end
    34	
    35	  def self.create_for(upload, width, height, opts = {})
    36	    return if width <= 0 || height <= 0
    37	    return if upload.try(:sha1).blank?
    38	
    39	    # no extension so try to guess it
    40	    upload.fix_image_extension if (!upload.extension)
    41	
    42	    if !upload.extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
    43	      if opts[:raise_on_error]
    44	        raise InvalidAccess
    45	      else
    46	        # nothing to do ... bad extension, not an image
    47	        return
    48	      end
    49	    end
    50	
    51	    # prefer to look up the thumbnail without grabbing any locks
    52	    extension = ".#{opts[:format] || upload.extension}"
    53	    thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
    54	
    55	    # correct bad thumbnail if needed
    56	    if thumbnail && (thumbnail.url.blank? || thumbnail.version != VERSION)
    57	      thumbnail.destroy!
    58	      thumbnail = nil
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    return thumbnail if thumbnail
    62	
    63	    store = Discourse.store
    64	
    65	    # create the thumbnail otherwise
    66	    original_path = store.path_for(upload)
    67	
    68	    if original_path.blank?
    69	      # download is protected with a DistributedMutex
    70	      original_path = store.download(upload)
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    if extension == ".svg" && upload.extension != "svg"
    74	      if opts[:raise_on_error]
    75	        raise InvalidAccess
    76	      else
    77	        # we can not convert any images to svg, unsupported
    78	        return
    79	      end
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    lock(upload.id, width, height) do
    83	      # may have been generated since we got the lock
    84	      thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
    85	
    86	      # return the previous thumbnail if any
    87	      return thumbnail if thumbnail
    88	
    89	      if original_path.blank?
    90	        Rails.logger.error("Could not find file in the store located at url: #{upload.url}")
    91	      else
    92	        # create a temp file with the same extension as the original
    93	
    94	        return nil if extension.length == 1
    95	
    96	        temp_file = Tempfile.new(["discourse-thumbnail", extension])
    97	        temp_path = temp_file.path
    98	
    99	        target_quality =
   100	          upload.target_image_quality(
   101	            original_path,
   102	            SiteSetting.ImageQuality.image_preview_jpg_quality,
   103	          )
   104	        opts = opts.merge(quality: target_quality) if target_quality
   105	        opts = opts.merge(upload_id: upload.id)
   106	
   107	        # special case, when "resizing" vectors we simply copy
   108	        if extension == ".svg"
   109	          FileUtils.cp(original_path, temp_path)
   110	          resized = true
   111	        elsif opts[:crop]
   112	          resized = crop(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
   113	        else
   114	          resized = resize(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
   115	        end
   116	
   117	        if resized
   118	          # TODO: crop vs resize should be stored in the db, quality should be stored
   119	          thumbnail =
   120	            OptimizedImage.create!(
   121	              upload_id: upload.id,
   122	              sha1: Upload.generate_digest(temp_path),
   123	              extension: extension,
   124	              width: width,
   125	              height: height,
   126	              url: "",
   127	              filesize: File.size(temp_path),
   128	              version: VERSION,
   129	            )
   130	
   131	          # store the optimized image and update its url
   132	          File.open(temp_path) do |file|
   133	            url = store.store_optimized_image(file, thumbnail, nil, secure: upload.secure?)
   134	            if url.present?
   135	              thumbnail.url = url
   136	              thumbnail.save
   137	            else
   138	              Rails.logger.error(
   139	                "Failed to store optimized image of size #{width}x#{height} from url: #{upload.url}\nTemp image path: #{temp_path}",
   140	              )
   141	            end
   142	          end
   143	        end
   144	
   145	        # close && remove temp file
   146	        temp_file.close!
   147	      end
   148	
   149	      thumbnail
   150	    end
   151	  end

   153	  def destroy
   154	    OptimizedImage.transaction do
   155	      Discourse.store.remove_optimized_image(self) if self.upload
   156	      super
   157	    end
   158	  end
   159	
   160	  def local?
   161	    !(url =~ %r{\A(https?:)?//})
   162	  end
   163	
   164	  def calculate_filesize
   165	    path =
   166	      if local?
   167	        Discourse.store.path_for(self)
   168	      else
   169	        Discourse.store.download!(self)
   170	      end
   171	    File.size(path)
   172	  end
   173	
   174	  def filesize
   175	    if size = read_attribute(:filesize)
   176	      size
   177	    else
   178	      size = calculate_filesize
   179	
   180	      self[:filesize] = size
   181	      update_columns(filesize: size) if !new_record?
   182	      size
   183	    end
   184	  end
   185	
   186	  def self.safe_path?(path)
   187	    # this matches instructions which call #to_s
   188	    path = path.to_s
   189	    return false if path != File.expand_path(path)
   190	    return false if path !~ %r{\A[\w\-\./]+\z}m
   191	    true
   192	  end
   193	
   194	  def self.ensure_safe_paths!(*paths)
   195	    paths.each { |path| raise Discourse::InvalidAccess unless safe_path?(path) }
   196	  end
   197	
   198	  IM_DECODERS = /\A(jpe?g|png|ico|gif|webp|avif|svg)\z/i
   199	
   200	  def self.prepend_decoder!(path, ext_path = nil, opts = nil)
   201	    opts ||= {}
   202	
   203	    # This logic is a little messy but the result of using mocks for most
   204	    # of the image tests. The idea here is you shouldn't trust the "original"
   205	    # path of a file to figure out its extension. However, in certain cases
   206	    # such as generating the loading upload thumbnail, we force the format,
   207	    # and this allows us to use the forced format in that case.
   208	    extension = nil
   209	    if (opts[:format] && path != ext_path)
   210	      extension = File.extname(path)[1..-1]
   211	    else
   212	      extension = File.extname(opts[:filename] || ext_path || path)[1..-1]
   213	    end
   214	
   215	    if !extension || !extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
   216	      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess.new("Unsupported extension: #{extension}")
   217	    end
   218	    "#{extension}:#{path}"
   219	  end
   220	
   221	  def self.thumbnail_or_resize
   222	    SiteSetting.strip_image_metadata ? "thumbnail" : "resize"
   223	  end
   224	
   225	  def self.resize_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
   226	    ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
   227	
   228	    # note FROM my not be named correctly
   229	    from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
   230	    to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
   231	
   232	    instructions = ["convert", "#{from}[0]"]
   233	
   234	    instructions << "-colors" << opts[:colors].to_s if opts[:colors]
   235	
   236	    instructions << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
   237	
   238	    # NOTE: ORDER is important!
   239	    instructions.concat(
   240	      %W[
   241	        -auto-orient
   242	        -gravity
   243	        center
   244	        -background
   245	        transparent
   246	        -#{thumbnail_or_resize}
   247	        #{dimensions}^
   248	        -extent
   249	        #{dimensions}
   250	        -interpolate
   251	        catrom
   252	        -unsharp
   253	        2x0.5+0.7+0
   254	        -interlace
   255	        none
   256	        -profile
   257	        #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}
   258	        #{to}
   259	      ],
   260	    )

     3	class OptimizedVideo < ActiveRecord::Base
     4	  belongs_to :upload
     5	  belongs_to :optimized_upload, class_name: "Upload"

    10	  validates :upload_id, uniqueness: { scope: :adapter }
    11	
    12	  def self.create_for(upload, filename, user_id, options = {})
    13	    return if upload.blank?
    14	
    15	    optimized_upload =
    16	      Upload.create!(
    17	        user_id: user_id,
    18	        original_filename: filename,
    19	        filesize: options[:filesize],
    20	        sha1: options[:sha1],
    21	        extension: options[:extension] || "mp4",
    22	        url: options[:url],
    23	        etag: options[:etag],
    24	        skip_video_conversion: true,
    25	        secure: upload.secure?,
    26	      )
    27	
    28	    optimized_video =
    29	      OptimizedVideo.new(
    30	        upload_id: upload.id,
    31	        optimized_upload_id: optimized_upload.id,
    32	        adapter: options[:adapter],
    33	      )
    34	
    35	    if optimized_video.save
    36	      UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [optimized_upload.id], target: upload)
    37	      optimized_video
    38	    else
    39	      optimized_upload.destroy
    40	      Rails.logger.error(
    41	        "Failed to create optimized video for upload ID #{upload.id}: #{optimized_video.errors.full_messages.join(", ")}",
    42	      )
    43	      nil
    44	    end
    45	  end
    46	
    47	  def destroy
    48	    OptimizedVideo.transaction do
    49	      Discourse.store.remove_upload(optimized_upload) if optimized_upload
    50	      if optimized_upload_id
    51	        UploadReference.where(upload_id: optimized_upload_id, target: upload).destroy_all
    52	      end
    53	      super
    54	      optimized_upload&.destroy
    55	    end
    56	  end
    57	
    58	  delegate :url, :filesize, :sha1, :extension, to: :optimized_upload

     3	require "fastimage"
     4	
     5	class UploadCreator
     6	  TYPES_TO_CROP = %w[avatar card_background custom_emoji profile_background].each(&:freeze)
     7	
     8	  ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS = %w[
     9	    circle
    10	    clipPath
    11	    defs
    12	    ellipse
    13	    feGaussianBlur
    14	    filter
    15	    g
    16	    line
    17	    linearGradient
    18	    marker
    19	    path
    20	    polygon
    21	    polyline
    22	    radialGradient
    23	    rect
    24	    stop
    25	    style
    26	    svg
    27	    text
    28	    textPath
    29	    tref
    30	    tspan
    31	    use
    32	  ].each(&:freeze)
    33	
    34	  # Available options
    35	  #  - type (string)
    36	  #  - origin (string)
    37	  #  - for_group_message (boolean)
    38	  #  - for_theme (boolean)
    39	  #  - for_private_message (boolean)
    40	  #  - pasted (boolean)
    41	  #  - for_export (boolean)
    42	  #  - for_gravatar (boolean)
    43	  #  - skip_validations (boolean)
    44	  def initialize(file, filename, opts = {})
    45	    @file = file
    46	    @filename = (filename || "").gsub(/[^[:print:]]/, "")
    47	    @upload = Upload.new(original_filename: @filename, filesize: 0)
    48	    @opts = opts
    49	    @filesize = @opts[:filesize] if @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
    50	    @opts[:validate] = (
    51	      if opts[:skip_validations].present?
    52	        !ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(opts[:skip_validations])
    53	      else
    54	        true
    55	      end
    56	    )
    57	  end
    58	
    59	  def create_for(user_id)
    60	    if filesize <= 0
    61	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
    62	      return @upload
    63	    end
    64	
    65	    @image_info =
    66	      begin
    67	        image = FastImage.new(@file)
    68	        image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
    69	        image
    70	      rescue StandardError
    71	        nil
    72	      end
    73	    is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@filename)
    74	    is_image ||= @image_info && FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
    75	    is_image = false if @opts[:for_theme]
    76	    is_thumbnail = SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && @opts[:type] == "thumbnail"
    77	
    78	    # If this is present then it means we are creating an upload record from
    79	    # an external_upload_stub and the file is > ExternalUploadManager::DOWNLOAD_LIMIT,
    80	    # so we have not downloaded it to a tempfile. no modifications can be made to the
    81	    # file in this case because it does not exist; we simply move it to its new location
    82	    # in S3
    83	    #
    84	    # FIXME: I've added a bunch of external_upload_too_big checks littered
    85	    # throughout the UploadCreator code. It would be better to have two separate
    86	    # classes with shared methods, rather than doing all these checks all over the
    87	    # place. Needs a refactor.
    88	    external_upload_too_big = @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
    89	    sha1_before_changes = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if @file
    90	
    91	    DistributedMutex.synchronize("upload_#{user_id}_#{@filename}") do
    92	      # We need to convert HEIFs early because FastImage does not consider them as images
    93	      if convert_heif_to_jpeg? && !external_upload_too_big
    94	        convert_heif!
    95	        is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
    96	      end
    97	
    98	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
    99	        extract_image_info!
   100	        return @upload if @upload.errors.present?
   101	
   102	        if @image_info.type == :svg
   103	          clean_svg!
   104	        elsif @image_info.type == :ico
   105	          convert_favicon_to_png!
   106	        elsif !Rails.env.test? || @opts[:force_optimize]
   107	          convert_to_jpeg! if convert_png_to_jpeg? || should_alter_quality?
   108	          fix_orientation! if should_fix_orientation?
   109	          crop! if should_crop?
   110	          optimize! if should_optimize?
   111	          downsize! if should_downsize?
   112	          return @upload if is_still_too_big?
   113	        end
   114	
   115	        # conversion may have switched the type
   116	        image_type = @image_info.type.to_s
   117	      end
   118	
   119	      # compute the sha of the file and generate a unique hash
   120	      # which is only used for secure uploads
   121	      sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if !external_upload_too_big
   122	      unique_hash = generate_fake_sha1_hash if SiteSetting.secure_uploads ||
   123	        external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail
   124	
   125	      # we do not check for duplicate uploads if secure uploads is
   126	      # enabled because we use a unique access hash to differentiate
   127	      # between uploads instead of the sha1, and to get around various
   128	      # access/permission issues for uploads
   129	      # We do not check for duplicate uploads for video thumbnails because
   130	      # their filename needs to match with their corresponding video. This also
   131	      # enables rebuilding the html on a topic to regenerate a thumbnail.
   132	      if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads && !external_upload_too_big && !is_thumbnail
   133	        # do we already have that upload?
   134	        @upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
   135	
   136	        # make sure the previous upload has not failed
   137	        if @upload && @upload.url.blank?
   138	          @upload.destroy
   139	          @upload = nil
   140	        end
   141	
   142	        # return the previous upload if any
   143	        if @upload
   144	          add_metadata!
   145	          UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
   146	          return @upload
   147	        end
   148	      end
   149	
   150	      fixed_original_filename = nil
   151	
   152	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
   153	        current_extension = File.extname(@filename).downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
   154	        expected_extension = ".#{image_type}".downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
   155	
   156	        # we have to correct original filename here, no choice
   157	        # otherwise validation will fail and we can not save
   158	        # TODO decide if we only run the validation on the extension
   159	        if current_extension != expected_extension
   160	          basename = File.basename(@filename, current_extension).presence || "image"
   161	          fixed_original_filename = "#{basename}#{expected_extension}"
   162	        end
   163	      end
   164	
   165	      # create the upload otherwise
   166	      @upload = Upload.new
   167	      @upload.user_id = user_id
   168	      @upload.original_filename = fixed_original_filename || @filename
   169	      @upload.filesize = filesize
   170	      @upload.sha1 =
   171	        (
   172	          if (SiteSetting.secure_uploads? || external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail)
   173	            unique_hash
   174	          else
   175	            sha1
   176	          end
   177	        )
   178	      @upload.original_sha1 = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? ? sha1 : nil
   179	      @upload.url = ""
   180	      @upload.origin = @opts[:origin][0...2000] if @opts[:origin]
   181	      @upload.extension = image_type || File.extname(@filename)[1..10]
   182	
   183	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
   184	        if @image_info.type.to_s == "svg"
   185	          w, h = [0, 0]
   186	
   187	          # identify can behave differently depending on how it's compiled and
   188	          # what programs (e.g. inkscape) are installed on your system.
   189	          # 'MSVG:' forces ImageMagick to use internal routines and behave
   190	          # consistently whether it's running from our docker container or not
   191	          begin
   192	            w, h =
   193	              Discourse::Utils
   194	                .execute_command(
   195	                  "identify",
   196	                  "-ping",
   197	                  "-format",
   198	                  "%w %h",
   199	                  "MSVG:#{@file.path}",
   200	                  timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
   201	                )
   202	                .split(" ")
   203	                .map(&:to_i)
   204	          rescue StandardError
   205	            # use default 0, 0
   206	          end
   207	        else
   208	          w, h = @image_info.size
   209	        end
   210	
   211	        @upload.thumbnail_width, @upload.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
   212	        @upload.width, @upload.height = w, h
   213	        @upload.animated = animated?
   214	        @upload.calculate_dominant_color!(@file.path)
   215	      end
   216	
   217	      add_metadata!
   218	
   219	      if SiteSetting.secure_uploads
   220	        secure, reason =
   221	          UploadSecurity.new(@upload, @opts.merge(creating: true)).should_be_secure_with_reason
   222	        attrs = @upload.secure_params(secure, reason, "upload creator")
   223	        @upload.assign_attributes(attrs)
   224	      end
   225	
   226	      # Callbacks using this event to validate the upload or the file must add errors to the
   227	      # upload errors object.
   228	      #
   229	      # Can't do any validation of the file if external_upload_too_big because we don't have
   230	      # the actual file.
   231	      if !external_upload_too_big
   232	        DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, @file, is_image, @upload, @opts[:validate])
   233	      end
   234	      return @upload unless @upload.errors.empty? && @upload.save(validate: @opts[:validate])
   235	
   236	      should_move = false
   237	      upload_changed =
   238	        if external_upload_too_big
   239	          false
   240	        else
   241	          Upload.generate_digest(@file) != sha1_before_changes
   242	        end
   243	
   244	      store = Discourse.store
   245	
   246	      if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key] && store.external?
   247	        should_move = external_upload_too_big || !upload_changed
   248	      end
   249	
   250	      if should_move
   251	        # move the file in the store instead of reuploading
   252	        url =
   253	          store.move_existing_stored_upload(
   254	            existing_external_upload_key: @opts[:existing_external_upload_key],
   255	            upload: @upload,
   256	          )
   257	      else
   258	        # store the file and update its url
   259	        File.open(@file.path) { |f| url = store.store_upload(f, @upload) }
   260	        if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
   261	          store.delete_file(@opts[:existing_external_upload_key])
   262	        end
   263	      end
   264	
   265	      if url.present?
   266	        @upload.url = url
   267	        @upload.save!(validate: @opts[:validate])
   268	      else
   269	        @upload.errors.add(
   270	          :url,
   271	          I18n.t("upload.store_failure", upload_id: @upload.id, user_id: user_id),
   272	        )
   273	      end
   274	
   275	      if @upload.errors.empty? && is_image && @opts[:type] == "avatar" && @upload.extension != "svg"
   276	        Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, upload_id: @upload.id)
   277	      end
   278	
   279	      if @upload.errors.empty?
   280	        UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
   281	      end
   282	
   283	      @upload
   284	    end
   285	  ensure
   286	    if @file
   287	      @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
   288	    end
   289	  end
   290	
   291	  def extract_image_info!
   292	    @image_info =
   293	      begin
   294	        image = FastImage.new(@file)
   295	        image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
   296	        image
   297	      rescue StandardError
   298	        nil
   299	      end
   300	    @file.rewind
   301	
   302	    if @image_info.nil?
   303	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.not_supported_or_corrupted"))
   304	    elsif filesize <= 0
   305	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
   306	    elsif pixels == 0 && @image_info.type.to_s != "svg"
   307	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.size_not_found"))
   308	    elsif max_image_pixels > 0 && pixels >= max_image_pixels
   309	      @upload.errors.add(
   310	        :base,
   311	        I18n.t(
   312	          "upload.images.larger_than_x_megapixels",
   313	          max_image_megapixels: SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels,
   314	          original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
   315	        ),
   316	      )
   317	    end
   318	  end
   319	
   320	  MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG = 1280 * 720
   321	
   322	  def convert_png_to_jpeg?
   323	    return false unless @image_info.type == :png
   324	    return true if @opts[:pasted]
   325	    return false if SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality == 100
   326	    pixels > MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG
   327	  end
   328	
   329	  MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED = 75_000

   332	  def convert_favicon_to_png!

   341	    to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)

   345	    opts = { flatten: false } # Preserve transparency
   346	
   347	    begin
   348	      execute_convert(from, to, opts)

   355	    @file = png_tempfile
   356	    extract_image_info!

   360	    return if @opts[:for_site_setting]

     3	class UploadMarkdown
     4	  def initialize(upload)
     5	    @upload = upload
     6	  end
     7	
     8	  def to_markdown(display_name: nil)
     9	    if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@upload.original_filename)
    10	      image_markdown(display_name: display_name)
    11	    elsif FileHelper.is_supported_playable_media?(@upload.original_filename)
    12	      playable_media_markdown(display_name: display_name)
    13	    else
    14	      attachment_markdown(display_name: display_name)
    15	    end
    16	  end
    17	
    18	  def image_markdown(display_name: nil)
    19	    display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
    20	    "![#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|#{@upload.width}x#{@upload.height}](#{@upload.short_url})"
    21	  end
    22	
    23	  def attachment_markdown(display_name: nil, with_filesize: true)
    24	    human_filesize = with_filesize ? " (#{@upload.human_filesize})" : ""
    25	    display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
    26	
    27	    "[#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|attachment](#{@upload.short_url})#{human_filesize}"
    28	  end
    29	
    30	  def playable_media_markdown(display_name: nil)
    31	    type =
    32	      if FileHelper.is_supported_audio?(@upload.original_filename)
    33	        "audio"
    34	      elsif FileHelper.is_supported_video?(@upload.original_filename)
    35	        "video"
    36	      end
    37	    return attachment_markdown if !type
    38	    display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
    39	    "![#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|#{type}](#{@upload.short_url})"
    40	  end
    41	
    42	  def self.escape_markdown(text)
    43	    text.gsub(/[\\*_~`\[\]|]/) { |c| "\\#{c}" }
    44	  end
    45	end

    94	    def download(*, **)

    99	
   100	    def download_safe(*, **)
   101	      Discourse.deprecate(
   102	        "`FileStore::BaseStore#download_safe` is deprecated. Use `#download` instead.",
   103	        since: "2026.02",
   104	        drop_from: "2026.08",
   105	      )
   106	      download(*, **)
   107	    end
   108	
   109	    def download!(*, **)
   110	      perform_download(*, **)
   111	    rescue StandardError
   112	      raise DownloadError
   113	    end
   114	
   115	    def purge_tombstone(grace_period)
   116	    end
   117	
   118	    def get_path_for(type, id, sha, extension)
   119	      depth = get_depth_for(id)
   120	      tree = File.join(*sha[0, depth].chars, "")
   121	      "#{type}/#{depth + 1}X/#{tree}#{sha}#{extension}"
   122	    end
   123	
   124	    def get_path_for_upload(upload)
   125	      # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
   126	      # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
   127	      if upload.url.present? && (path = upload.url[UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX, 1])
   128	        return prefix_path(path)
   129	      end
   130	
   131	      extension =
   132	        if upload.extension
   133	          ".#{upload.extension}"
   134	        else
   135	          # Maintain backward compatibility before Jobs::MigrateUploadExtensions runs
   136	          File.extname(upload.original_filename)
   137	        end
   138	
   139	      get_path_for("original", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
   140	    end
   141	
   142	    def get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
   143	      # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
   144	      # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
   145	      if optimized_image.url.present? && (path = optimized_image.url[OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX, 1])
   146	        return prefix_path(path)
   147	      end
   148	
   149	      upload = optimized_image.upload
   150	      version = optimized_image.version || 1
   151	      extension =
   152	        "_#{version}_#{optimized_image.width}x#{optimized_image.height}#{optimized_image.extension}"
   153	      get_path_for("optimized", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
   154	    end
   155	
   156	    CACHE_DIR = "#{Rails.root}/tmp/download_cache/"
   157	    CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE = 500
   158	    CACHE_EVICT_COUNT = 100
   159	
   160	    def get_cache_path_for(filename)
   161	      "#{CACHE_DIR}#{filename}"
   162	    end
   163	
   164	    def get_from_cache(filename)
   165	      path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
   166	      path if File.exist?(path)
   167	    end
   168	
   169	    def cache_file(file, filename)
   170	      path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
   171	      dir = File.dirname(path)
   172	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
   173	      FileUtils.cp(file.path, path)
   174	
   175	      DiskCacheEviction.evict(
   176	        dir: CACHE_DIR,
   177	        max_entries: CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE,
   178	        evict_count: CACHE_EVICT_COUNT,
   179	      )
   180	    end
   181	
   182	    private
   183	
   184	    def perform_download(object, max_file_size_kb: nil)
   185	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("download_#{object.sha1}", validity: 3.minutes) do
   186	        name = object.respond_to?(:original_filename) ? object.original_filename : object.url
   187	        extension = File.extname(name)
   188	        filename = "#{object.sha1}#{extension}"
   189	
   190	        unless File.exist?(get_cache_path_for(filename))
   191	          max_file_size_kb ||= [
   192	            SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
   193	            SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
   194	          ].max.kilobytes
   195	
   196	          if object.respond_to?(:secure) ? object.secure? : object.upload.secure?
   197	            url =
   198	              Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(object.url, include_content_disposition: false)
   199	          else
   200	            url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(object.url)
   201	          end
   202	
   203	          url = "#{SiteSetting.scheme}:#{url}" if url =~ %r{\A//}
   204	
   205	          file =
   206	            FileHelper.download(
   207	              url,
   208	              max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
   209	              tmp_file_name: "discourse-download",
   210	              follow_redirect: true,
   211	            )
   212	
   213	          return if file.nil?
   214	
   215	          cache_file(file, filename)
   216	        end
   217	
   218	        get_from_cache(filename)
   219	      end
   220	    end
   221	
   222	    def not_implemented
   223	      raise "Not implemented."
   224	    end
   225	
   226	    def get_depth_for(id)
   227	      depths = [0]
   228	      depths << Math.log(id / 1_000.0, 16).ceil if id.positive?
   229	      depths.max
   230	    end
   231	
   232	    def prefix_path(path)
   233	      path
   234	    end
   235	  end
   236	end

     5	module FileStore
     6	  class LocalStore < BaseStore
     7	    def store_file(file, path)

     9	      "#{Discourse.base_path}#{path}"
    10	    end

    12	    def remove_file(url, _)
    13	      return unless is_relative?(url)
    14	      source = "#{public_dir}#{url}"
    15	      return unless File.exist?(source)
    16	      destination = "#{public_dir}#{url.sub("/uploads/", "/uploads/tombstone/")}"
    17	      dir = Pathname.new(destination).dirname
    18	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
    19	      FileUtils.remove(destination) if File.exist?(destination)
    20	      FileUtils.move(source, destination, force: true)
    21	      FileUtils.touch(destination)
    22	    end
    23	
    24	    def has_been_uploaded?(url)
    25	      is_relative?(url) || is_local?(url)
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    def absolute_base_url
    29	      "#{Discourse.base_url_no_prefix}#{relative_base_url}"
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    def absolute_base_cdn_url
    33	      "#{Discourse.asset_host}#{relative_base_url}"
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    def relative_base_url
    37	      File.join(Discourse.base_path, upload_path)
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    def temporary_upload_path(filename)
    41	      FileStore::BaseStore.temporary_upload_path(filename, folder_prefix: relative_base_url)
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    def external?
    45	      false
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    def download_url(upload)
    49	      return unless upload
    50	      File.join(relative_base_url, upload.sha1)
    51	    end
    52	
    53	    def cdn_url(url)

    55	    end
    56	
    57	    def path_for(upload)
    58	      url = upload.try(:url)
    59	      "#{public_dir}#{upload.url}" if url && url[0] == "/" && url[1] != "/"
    60	    end
    61	
    62	    def purge_tombstone(grace_period)
    63	      if Dir.exist?(Discourse.store.tombstone_dir)
    64	        Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
    65	          "find",
    66	          tombstone_dir,
    67	          "-mtime",
    68	          "+#{grace_period}",
    69	          "-type",
    70	          "f",
    71	          "-delete",
    72	        )
    73	      end
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    def get_path_for(type, upload_id, sha, extension)
    77	      prefix_path(super(type, upload_id, sha, extension))
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    def copy_file(file, path)
    81	      dir = Pathname.new(path).dirname
    82	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
    83	      # move the file to the right location
    84	      # not using mv, cause permissions are no good on move
    85	      File.open(path, "wb") { |f| f.write(file.read) }
    86	    end
    87	
    88	    def is_relative?(url)
    89	      url.present? && url.start_with?(relative_base_url)
    90	    end
    91	
    92	    def is_local?(url)
    93	      return false if url.blank?
    94	      absolute_url = url.start_with?("//") ? SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + url : url
    95	      absolute_url.start_with?(absolute_base_url) || absolute_url.start_with?(absolute_base_cdn_url)
    96	    end
    97	
    98	    def public_dir
    99	      File.join(Rails.root, "public")
   100	    end
   101	
   102	    def tombstone_dir
   103	      "#{public_dir}#{relative_base_url.sub("/uploads/", "/uploads/tombstone/")}"
   104	    end
   105	
   106	    def list_missing_uploads(skip_optimized: false)
   107	      list_missing(Upload)
   108	      list_missing(OptimizedImage) unless skip_optimized
   109	    end
   110	
   111	    def copy_from(source_path)
   112	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.join(public_dir, upload_path))
   113	
   114	      Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
   115	        "rsync",
   116	        "-a",
   117	        "--safe-links",
   118	        "#{source_path}/",
   119	        "#{upload_path}/",
   120	        failure_message: "Failed to copy uploads.",
   121	        chdir: public_dir,
   122	      )
   123	    end
   124	
   125	    private
   126	
   127	    def list_missing(model)
   128	      count = 0
   129	      model.find_each do |upload|
   130	        # could be a remote image
   131	        next unless upload.url =~ %r{\A/[^/]}
   132	
   133	        path = "#{public_dir}#{upload.url}"
   134	        bad = true
   135	        begin
   136	          bad = false if File.size(path) != 0
   137	        rescue StandardError
   138	          # something is messed up
   139	        end
   140	        if bad
   141	          count += 1
   142	          puts path
   143	        end
   144	      end
   145	      puts "#{count} of #{model.count} #{model.name.underscore.pluralize} are missing" if count > 0
   146	    end
   147	
   148	    def prefix_path(path)
   149	      File.join("/", upload_path, path)
   150	    end
   151	  end
   152	end

     3	require "uri"
     4	require "mini_mime"
     5	require "file_store/base_store"
     6	require "s3_helper"
     7	require "file_helper"
     8	
     9	module FileStore
    10	  class S3Store < BaseStore
    11	    TOMBSTONE_PREFIX = "tombstone/"
    12	
    13	    delegate :abort_multipart,
    14	             :presign_multipart_part,
    15	             :list_multipart_parts,
    16	             :complete_multipart,
    17	             to: :s3_helper
    18	
    19	    def initialize(s3_helper = nil)
    20	      @s3_helper = s3_helper
    21	    end
    22	
    23	    def s3_helper
    24	      @s3_helper ||=
    25	        S3Helper.new(
    26	          s3_bucket,
    27	          Rails.configuration.multisite ? multisite_tombstone_prefix : TOMBSTONE_PREFIX,
    28	          use_accelerate_endpoint: SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_transfer_acceleration,
    29	        )
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    def store_upload(file, upload, content_type = nil)
    33	      upload.url = nil
    34	      path = get_path_for_upload(upload)
    35	      url, upload.etag =
    36	        store_file(
    37	          file,
    38	          path,
    39	          filename: upload.original_filename,
    40	          content_type: content_type,
    41	          cache_locally: true,
    42	          private: upload.secure?,
    43	          upload_id: upload.id,
    44	        )
    45	      url
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    def move_existing_stored_upload(existing_external_upload_key:, upload: nil, content_type: nil)
    49	      upload.url = nil
    50	      path = get_path_for_upload(upload)
    51	      url, upload.etag =
    52	        store_file(
    53	          nil,
    54	          path,
    55	          filename: upload.original_filename,
    56	          content_type: content_type,
    57	          cache_locally: false,
    58	          private: upload.secure?,
    59	          move_existing: true,
    60	          existing_external_upload_key: existing_external_upload_key,
    61	        )
    62	      url
    63	    end
    64	
    65	    def store_optimized_image(file, optimized_image, content_type = nil, secure: false)
    66	      optimized_image.url = nil
    67	      path = get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
    68	      url, optimized_image.etag =
    69	        store_file(file, path, content_type: content_type, private: secure)
    70	      url
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    # File is an actual Tempfile on disk
    74	    #
    75	    # An existing_external_upload_key is given for cases where move_existing is specified.
    76	    # This is an object already uploaded directly to S3 that we are now moving
    77	    # to its final resting place with the correct sha and key.
    78	    #
    79	    # options
    80	    #   - filename
    81	    #   - content_type
    82	    #   - cache_locally
    83	    #   - move_existing
    84	    #   - private
    85	    #   - existing_external_upload_key
    86	    def store_file(file, path, opts = {})
    87	      path = path.dup
    88	
    89	      filename = opts[:filename].presence || File.basename(path)
    90	      # cache file locally when needed
    91	      cache_file(file, File.basename(path)) if opts[:cache_locally]
    92	
    93	      cache_control = "max-age=#{SiteSetting.s3_max_age}, public, immutable"
    94	      if SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate != SiteSetting.defaults[:s3_stale_while_revalidate]
    95	        cache_control =
    96	          "#{cache_control}, stale-while-revalidate=#{SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate}"
    97	      end
    98	
    99	      options = {
   100	        cache_control: cache_control,
   101	        content_type:
   102	          opts[:content_type].presence || MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(filename)&.content_type,
   103	      }.merge(default_s3_options(secure: opts[:private]))
   104	
   105	      # Only serve inline for allowlisted safe file types (non-SVG images and PDFs)
   106	      # to prevent XSS via HTML/XML/SVG uploads. All other files force download.
   107	      # See https://github.com/discourse/discourse/commit/31e31ef44973dc4daaee2f010d71588ea5873b53
   108	      options[:content_disposition] = self.class.content_disposition_for(filename)
   109	
   110	      path.prepend(File.join(upload_path, "/")) if Rails.configuration.multisite
   111	
   112	      # if this fails, it will throw an exception
   113	      if opts[:move_existing] && opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
   114	        original_path = opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
   115	        options[:apply_metadata_to_destination] = true
   116	        path, etag = s3_helper.copy(original_path, path, options: options)
   117	        delete_file(original_path)
   118	      else
   119	        path, etag = s3_helper.upload(file, path, options)
   120	      end
   121	
   122	      # return the upload url and etag
   123	      [File.join(absolute_base_url, path), etag]
   124	    end
   125	
   126	    def delete_file(path)
   127	      # delete the object outright without moving to tombstone,
   128	      # not recommended for most use cases
   129	      s3_helper.delete_object(path)
   130	    end
   131	
   132	    def remove_file(url, path)
   133	      return unless has_been_uploaded?(url)
   134	      # copy the removed file to tombstone
   135	      s3_helper.remove(path, true)
   136	    end
   137	
   138	    def copy_file(source:, destination:, secure:)
   139	      s3_helper.copy(source, destination, options: default_s3_options(secure:))
   140	    end
   141	
   142	    def has_been_uploaded?(url)
   143	      return false if url.blank?
   144	
   145	      begin
   146	        parsed_url = URI.parse(UrlHelper.encode(url))
   147	      rescue StandardError
   148	        # There are many exceptions possible here including Addressable::URI:: exceptions
   149	        # and URI:: exceptions, catch all may seem wide, but it makes no sense to raise ever
   150	        # on an invalid url here
   151	        return false
   152	      end
   153	
   154	      base_hostname = URI.parse(absolute_base_url).hostname
   155	      if url[base_hostname]
   156	        # if the hostnames match it means the upload is in the same
   157	        # bucket on s3. however, the bucket folder path may differ in
   158	        # some cases, and we do not want to assume the url is uploaded
   159	        # here. e.g. the path of the current site could be /prod and the
   160	        # other site could be /staging
   161	        if s3_bucket_folder_path.present?
   162	          return parsed_url.path.starts_with?("/#{s3_bucket_folder_path}")
   163	        else
   164	          return true
   165	        end
   166	      end
   167	
   168	      return false if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.blank?
   169	
   170	      s3_cdn_url = URI.parse(SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url || "")
   171	      cdn_hostname = s3_cdn_url.hostname
   172	
   173	      if cdn_hostname.presence && url[cdn_hostname] &&
   174	           (s3_cdn_url.path.blank? || parsed_url.path.starts_with?(s3_cdn_url.path))
   175	        return true
   176	      end
   177	      false
   178	    end
   179	
   180	    def s3_bucket_folder_path
   181	      S3Helper.get_bucket_and_folder_path(s3_bucket)[1]
   182	    end
   183	
   184	    def s3_bucket_name
   185	      S3Helper.get_bucket_and_folder_path(s3_bucket)[0]
   186	    end
   187	
   188	    def absolute_base_url
   189	      @absolute_base_url ||= SiteSetting.Upload.absolute_base_url
   190	    end
   191	
   192	    def s3_upload_host
   193	      SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.presence || "https:#{absolute_base_url}"
   194	    end
   195	
   196	    def external?
   197	      true
   198	    end
   199	
   200	    def purge_tombstone(grace_period)
   201	      s3_helper.update_tombstone_lifecycle(grace_period)
   202	    end
   203	
   204	    def multisite_tombstone_prefix
   205	      File.join("uploads", "tombstone", RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db, "/")
   206	    end
   207	
   208	    def download_url(upload)
   209	      return unless upload
   210	      "#{upload.short_path}?dl=1"
   211	    end
   212	
   213	    def path_for(upload)
   214	      url = upload&.url
   215	      FileStore::LocalStore.new.path_for(upload) if url && url[%r{\A/[^/]}]
   216	    end
   217	
   218	    def url_for(upload, force_download: false)
   219	      if upload.secure? || force_download
   220	        presigned_get_url(
   221	          get_upload_key(upload),
   222	          force_download: force_download,
   223	          filename: upload.original_filename,
   224	        )
   225	      elsif SiteSetting.s3_use_cdn_url_for_all_uploads
   226	        cdn_url(upload.url)
   227	      else
   228	        upload.url
   229	      end
   230	    end
   231	
   232	    def cdn_url(url)
   233	      return url if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.blank?
   234	      schema = url[%r{\A(https?:)?//}, 1]
   235	      folder = s3_bucket_folder_path.nil? ? "" : "#{s3_bucket_folder_path}/"
   236	      url.sub(
   237	        File.join("#{schema}#{absolute_base_url}", folder),
   238	        File.join(SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url, "/"),
   239	      )
   240	    end
   241	
   242	    def signed_url_for_path(
   243	      path,
   244	      expires_in: SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds,
   245	      force_download: false,
   246	      filename: nil,
   247	      include_content_disposition:
   248	    )
   249	      key = path.sub(absolute_base_url + "/", "")
   250	
   251	      presigned_get_url(
   252	        key,
   253	        expires_in:,
   254	        force_download:,
   255	        filename: include_content_disposition ? (filename || File.basename(path)) : nil,
   256	      )
   257	    end
   258	
   259	    def signed_request_for_temporary_upload(
   260	      file_name,
   261	      expires_in: S3Helper::UPLOAD_URL_EXPIRES_AFTER_SECONDS,
   262	      metadata: {}
   263	    )
   264	      key = temporary_upload_path(file_name)
   265	
   266	      s3_helper.presigned_request(
   267	        key,
   268	        method: :put_object,
   269	        expires_in: expires_in,
   270	        opts: { metadata: metadata }.merge(default_s3_options(secure: true)),
   271	      )
   272	    end
   273	
   274	    def temporary_upload_path(file_name)
   275	      folder_prefix =
   276	        s3_bucket_folder_path.nil? ? upload_path : File.join(s3_bucket_folder_path, upload_path)
   277	      FileStore::BaseStore.temporary_upload_path(file_name, folder_prefix: folder_prefix)
   278	    end
   279	
   280	    def object_from_path(path)
   281	      s3_helper.object(path)
   282	    end
   283	
   284	    def cache_avatar(avatar, user_id)
   285	      source = avatar.url.sub(absolute_base_url + "/", "")
   286	      destination = avatar_template(avatar, user_id).sub(absolute_base_url + "/", "")
   287	      s3_helper.copy(source, destination)
   288	    end
   289	
   290	    def avatar_template(avatar, user_id)
   291	      UserAvatar.external_avatar_url(user_id, avatar.upload_id, avatar.width)
   292	    end
   293	
   294	    def s3_bucket
   295	      if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_upload_bucket.blank?
   296	        raise Discourse::SiteSettingMissing.new("s3_upload_bucket")
   297	      end
   298	      SiteSetting.Upload.s3_upload_bucket.downcase
   299	    end

   301	    def list_missing_uploads(skip_optimized: false)
   302	      if s3_inventory_bucket = SiteSetting.s3_inventory_bucket
   303	        s3_options = {}
   304	
   305	        if (s3_inventory_bucket_region = SiteSetting.s3_inventory_bucket_region).present?
   306	          s3_options[:region] = s3_inventory_bucket_region
   307	        end
   308	
   309	        S3Inventory.new(:upload, s3_inventory_bucket:, s3_options:).backfill_etags_and_list_missing
   310	
   311	        unless skip_optimized
   312	          S3Inventory.new(:optimized, s3_inventory_bucket:).backfill_etags_and_list_missing
   313	        end
   314	      else
   315	        list_missing(Upload.by_users, "original/")
   316	        list_missing(OptimizedImage, "optimized/") unless skip_optimized
   317	      end
   318	    end
   319	
   320	    def update_upload_access_control(upload, remove_existing_acl: false)
   321	      key = get_upload_key(upload)
   322	      update_access_control(key, upload.secure?, remove_existing_acl:)
   323	
   324	      upload.optimized_images.each do |optimized_image|
   325	        update_optimized_image_access_control(

   327	          secure: upload.secure,
   328	          remove_existing_acl:,

   332	      true
   333	    end

   336	      optimized_image,
   337	      secure: false,
   338	      remove_existing_acl: false
   339	    )
   340	      optimized_image_key = get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	##
     4	# A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
     5	#
     6	# Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
     7	# are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
     8	# when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
     9	# option is present.
    10	#
    11	# If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
    12	# secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
    13	# original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
    14	# post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
    15	# on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
    16	# complex queries and joins to perform.
    17	#
    18	# These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
    19	# an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
    20	# (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
    21	# state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
    22	#
    23	# NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
    24	# secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
    25	# does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
    26	# efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
    27	class UploadSecurity
    28	  @@custom_public_types = []
    29	
    30	  PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
    31	    avatar
    32	    custom_emoji
    33	    profile_background
    34	    card_background
    35	    category_logo
    36	    category_logo_dark
    37	    category_background
    38	    group_flair
    39	    badge_image
    40	    site_setting
    41	  ]
    42	
    43	  PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
    44	    Badge
    45	    Category
    46	    CustomEmoji
    47	    Group
    48	    SiteSetting
    49	    ThemeField
    50	    User
    51	    UserAvatar
    52	    UserProfile
    53	  ]
    54	
    55	  def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
    56	    @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
    57	  end
    58	
    59	  # used in tests
    60	  def self.reset_custom_public_types
    61	    @@custom_public_types = []
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  def initialize(upload, opts = {})
    65	    @upload = upload
    66	    @opts = opts
    67	    @upload_type = @opts[:type]
    68	    @creating = @opts[:creating]
    69	  end
    70	
    71	  def should_be_secure?
    72	    should_be_secure_with_reason.first
    73	  end
    74	
    75	  def should_be_secure_with_reason
    76	    insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
    77	    secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
    78	      return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
    79	      return true, reason if perform_check(check)
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    [false, "no checks satisfied"]
    83	  end
    84	
    85	  private
    86	
    87	  def access_control_post
    88	    @access_control_post ||=
    89	      @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def insecure_context_checks
    93	    {
    94	      secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
    95	      insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
    96	      public_type: "upload is public type",
    97	      regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
    98	      publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
    99	    }
   100	  end
   101	
   102	  def secure_context_checks
   103	    {
   104	      login_required: "login is required",
   105	      access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
   106	      secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
   107	      uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
   108	      already_secure: "upload is already secure",
   109	    }
   110	  end
   111	
   112	  # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
   113	  # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
   114	  # immediately if there is an access control post.
   115	  def priority_check?(check)
   116	    check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
   117	  end
   118	
   119	  def perform_check(check)
   120	    send("#{check}_check")
   121	  end
   122	
   123	  #### START PUBLIC CHECKS ####
   124	
   125	  def secure_uploads_disabled_check
   126	    !SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
   127	  end
   128	
   129	  def insecure_creation_for_modifiers_check
   130	    return false if !@creating
   131	    @upload.for_theme || @upload.for_site_setting || @upload.for_gravatar
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  def public_type_check
   135	    PUBLIC_TYPES.include?(@upload_type) || @@custom_public_types.include?(@upload_type)
   136	  end
   137	
   138	  def publicly_referenced_first_check
   139	    return false if @creating
   140	    first_reference =
   141	      @upload
   142	        .upload_references
   143	        .joins(<<~SQL)
   144	          LEFT JOIN posts ON upload_references.target_type = 'Post' AND upload_references.target_id = posts.id
   145	        SQL
   146	        .where("posts.deleted_at IS NULL")
   147	        .order("upload_references.created_at ASC, upload_references.id ASC")
   148	        .first
   149	    return false if first_reference.blank?
   150	    PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES.include?(first_reference.target_type)
   151	  end
   152	
   153	  def regular_emoji_check
   154	    return false if @upload.origin.blank?
   155	    uri = URI.parse(@upload.origin)
   156	    return true if Emoji.all.map(&:url).include?("#{uri.path}?#{uri.query}")
   157	    uri.path.include?("images/emoji")
   158	  end
   159	
   160	  #### END PUBLIC CHECKS ####
   161	
   162	  #--------------------------#
   163	
   164	  #### START PRIVATE CHECKS ####
   165	
   166	  def login_required_check
   167	    SiteSetting.login_required? && !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_pm_only?
   168	  end
   169	
   170	  # Whether the upload should remain secure or not after posting depends on its context,
   171	  # which is based on the post it is linked to via access_control_post_id.
   172	  #
   173	  # If that post should_secure_uploads? then the upload should also be secure.
   174	  #
   175	  # This may change to false if the upload was set to secure on upload e.g. in
   176	  # a post composer then it turned out that the post itself was not in a secure context.
   177	  #
   178	  # A post is with secure uploads if it is a private message or in a read restricted
   179	  # category. See `Post#should_secure_uploads?` for the full definition.
   180	  def access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check
   181	    access_control_post&.should_secure_uploads?
   182	  end
   183	
   184	  def uploading_in_composer_check
   185	    @upload_type == "composer"
   186	  end
   187	
   188	  def secure_creation_for_modifiers_check
   189	    return false if !@creating
   190	    @upload.for_private_message || @upload.for_group_message
   191	  end
   192	
   193	  def already_secure_check
   194	    @upload.secure?
   195	  end
   196	
   197	  #### END PRIVATE CHECKS ####
   198	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module SecureUploadEndpointHelpers
     4	  include ActiveSupport::Concern
     5	
     6	  def upload_from_path_and_extension(path_with_ext)
     7	    sha1 = File.basename(path_with_ext, File.extname(path_with_ext))
     8	    # this takes care of optimized image requests
     9	    sha1 = sha1.partition("_").first if sha1.include?("_")
    10	    Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
    11	  end
    12	
    13	  def upload_from_full_url(url)
    14	    Upload.find_by(sha1: Upload.sha1_from_long_url(url))
    15	  end
    16	
    17	  def check_secure_upload_permission(upload)
    18	    if upload.access_control_post_id.present?
    19	      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if current_user.nil? && SiteSetting.login_required
    20	      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if !guardian.can_see?(upload.access_control_post)
    21	    else
    22	      raise Discourse::NotFound if current_user.nil?
    23	    end
    24	  end
    25	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	require "mini_mime"
     4	
     5	class UploadsController < ApplicationController
     6	  include ExternalUploadHelpers
     7	  include SecureUploadEndpointHelpers
     8	
     9	  requires_login except: %i[show show_short _show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
    10	
    11	  skip_before_action :preload_json,
    12	                     :check_xhr,
    13	                     :redirect_to_login_if_required,
    14	                     :redirect_to_profile_if_required,
    15	                     only: %i[show show_short _show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
    16	  protect_from_forgery except: :show
    17	
    18	  before_action :is_asset_path,
    19	                :apply_cdn_headers,
    20	                only: %i[show show_short _show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
    21	  before_action :external_store_check, only: %i[_show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
    22	
    23	  SECURE_REDIRECT_GRACE_SECONDS = 5
    24	
    25	  def create
    26	    # capture current user for block later on
    27	    me = current_user
    28	    RateLimiter.new(
    29	      current_user,
    30	      "uploads-per-minute",
    31	      SiteSetting.max_uploads_per_minute,
    32	      1.minute,
    33	    ).performed!
    34	
    35	    type =
    36	      if params[:upload_type].presence
    37	        params[:upload_type]
    38	      elsif params[:type].presence
    39	        Discourse.deprecate(
    40	          "the :type param of `POST /uploads` is deprecated, use the :upload_type param instead",
    41	          since: "3.4",
    42	          drop_from: "3.5",
    43	        )
    44	        params[:type]
    45	      else
    46	        params.require(:upload_type)
    47	      end
    48	    # 50 characters ought to be enough for the upload type
    49	    type = type.parameterize(separator: "_")[0..50]
    50	
    51	    if type == "avatar" &&
    52	         (
    53	           SiteSetting.discourse_connect_overrides_avatar || SiteSetting.auth_overrides_avatar ||
    54	             !me.in_any_groups?(SiteSetting.uploaded_avatars_allowed_groups_map)
    55	         )
    56	      return render json: failed_json, status: :unprocessable_entity
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    url = params[:url]
    60	    file = params[:file] || params[:files]&.first
    61	    pasted = params[:pasted] == "true"
    62	    for_private_message = params[:for_private_message] == "true"
    63	    for_site_setting = params[:for_site_setting] == "true"
    64	    site_setting_name = for_site_setting ? params[:site_setting_name] : nil
    65	    is_api = is_api?
    66	    retain_hours = params[:retain_hours].to_i
    67	
    68	    # note, atm hijack is processed in its own context and has not access to controller
    69	    # longer term we may change this
    70	    hijack do
    71	      begin
    72	        info =
    73	          UploadsController.create_upload(
    74	            current_user: me,
    75	            file:,
    76	            url:,
    77	            type:,
    78	            for_private_message:,
    79	            for_site_setting:,
    80	            site_setting_name:,
    81	            pasted:,
    82	            is_api:,
    83	            retain_hours:,
    84	          )
    85	      rescue => e
    86	        render json: failed_json.merge(message: e.message&.split("\n")&.first),
    87	               status: :unprocessable_entity
    88	      else
    89	        render json: UploadsController.serialize_upload(info), status: Upload === info ? 200 : 422
    90	      end
    91	    end
    92	  end
    93	
    94	  def lookup_urls
    95	    params.permit(short_urls: [])
    96	    uploads = []
    97	
    98	    if (params[:short_urls] && params[:short_urls].length > 0)
    99	      PrettyText::Helpers
   100	        .lookup_upload_urls(params[:short_urls])
   101	        .each do |short_url, paths|
   102	          uploads << { short_url: short_url, url: paths[:url], short_path: paths[:short_path] }
   103	        end
   104	    end
   105	
   106	    render json: uploads.to_json
   107	  end
   108	
   109	  def show
   110	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   111	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   112	
   113	    return render_404 if !RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.has_db?(params[:site])
   114	
   115	    begin
   116	      request_site_current_user =
   117	        request.env.delete(Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY)
   118	
   119	      RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.with_connection(params[:site]) do |db|
   120	        begin
   121	          # current_user here refers to the user for the site that we are operating on
   122	          # using with_connection. If DB for the target site matches the current site
   123	          # for the request, then current_user will be the same as the request_site_current_user
   124	          return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
   125	
   126	          upload =
   127	            Upload.find_by(sha1: params[:sha]) ||
   128	              Upload.find_by(id: params[:id], url: request.env["PATH_INFO"])
   129	
   130	          if upload.present?
   131	            if !Discourse.store.internal?
   132	              local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
   133	              return render_404 unless local_store.has_been_uploaded?(upload.url)
   134	            end
   135	
   136	            send_file_local_upload(upload)
   137	          else
   138	            render_404
   139	          end
   140	        end
   141	      end
   142	    ensure
   143	      request.env[Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY] = request_site_current_user
   144	    end
   145	  end
   146	
   147	  def show_short
   148	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   149	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   150	
   151	    return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
   152	
   153	    sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(params[:base62])
   154	
   155	    if upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
   156	      return handle_secure_upload_request(upload) if upload.secure? && SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
   157	
   158	      if Discourse.store.internal?
   159	        send_file_local_upload(upload)
   160	      else
   161	        redirect_to Discourse.store.url_for(upload, force_download: force_download?),
   162	                    allow_other_host: true
   163	      end
   164	    else
   165	      render_404
   166	    end
   167	  end
   168	
   169	  # Kept to avoid rebaking old posts with /show-secure-uploads/ in their
   170	  # contents, this will ensure the uploads in these posts continue to
   171	  # work in future.
   172	  def _show_secure_deprecated
   173	    show_secure
   174	  end
   175	
   176	  def show_secure
   177	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   178	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   179	
   180	    path_with_ext =
   181	      params[:extension].nil? ? params[:path] : "#{params[:path]}.#{params[:extension]}"

nl: app/controllers/uploaded_images_controller.rb: No such file or directory

Creation and access are split: `UploadsController` does request handling, `UploadCreator` mutates/validates/stores the file, `FileStore::*` owns path and URL generation, and asynchronous work fans out through jobs after the Upload row exists. I’m now collecting those jobs, cook/inline references, and non-obvious polymorphic `UploadReference` users.

content.text: A text search alone is noisy, so I’m using it as an index and then reading the high-signal files around each match. The key dependency patterns so far are: explicit `belongs_to/has_many`, polymorphic references, raw/cooked short URL extraction, storage URL/path helpers, and background jobs that assume `id`, `sha1`, `url`, `secure`, and derived records stay coherent.

   309	            Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
   310	              "identify",
   311	              "-ping",
   312	              "-format",
   313	              "%w %h",
   314	              path,
   315	              timeout: MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
   316	            ).split(" ")

   319	          end

   321	        w, h = FastImage.new(path, raise_on_failure: true).size
   322	      end

   324	      self.width = w || 0
   325	      self.height = h || 0
   326	
   327	      self.thumbnail_width, self.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
   328	
   329	      self.update_columns(
   330	        width: width,
   331	        height: height,
   332	        thumbnail_width: thumbnail_width,
   333	        thumbnail_height: thumbnail_height,
   334	      )
   335	    rescue => e
   336	      Discourse.warn_exception(e, message: "Error getting image dimensions")
   337	    end
   338	    nil
   339	  end

   341	  # on demand image size calculation, this allows us to null out image sizes
   342	  # and still handle as needed
   343	  def get_dimension(key)
   344	    if v = self[key]
   345	      return v
   346	    end
   347	    fix_dimensions!
   348	    self[key]

   367	  def dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: false)
   368	    val = read_attribute(:dominant_color)
   369	    if val.nil? && calculate_if_missing
   370	      calculate_dominant_color!
   371	      read_attribute(:dominant_color)
   372	    else
   373	      val
   374	    end
   375	  end

   377	  def calculate_dominant_color!(local_path = nil)
   378	    color = nil
   379	
   380	    color = "" if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}") || extension == "svg"
   381	
   382	    if color.nil?
   383	      local_path ||=
   384	        if local?
   385	          Discourse.store.path_for(self)
   386	        else
   387	          Discourse.store.download(self)
   388	        end
   389	
   390	      if local_path.nil?
   391	        # Download failed. Could be too large to download, or file could be missing in s3
   392	        color = ""
   393	      end
   394	
   395	      color ||=
   396	        begin
   397	          data =
   398	            Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
   399	              "nice",
   400	              "-n",
   401	              "10",
   402	              "convert",
   403	              local_path,
   404	              "-depth",
   405	              "8",
   406	              "-resize",
   407	              "1x1",
   408	              "-define",
   409	              "histogram:unique-colors=true",
   410	              "-format",
   411	              "%c",
   412	              "histogram:info:",
   413	              timeout: DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
   414	            )
   415	
   416	          # Output format:
   417	          # 1: (110.873,116.226,93.8821) #6F745E srgb(43.4798%,45.5789%,36.8165%)
   418	
   419	          color = data[/#([0-9A-F]{6})/, 1]
   420	
   421	          raise "Calculated dominant color but unable to parse output:\n#{data}" if color.nil?
   422	
   423	          color
   424	        rescue Discourse::Utils::CommandError
   425	          # Timeout or unable to parse image
   426	          # This can happen due to bad user input - ignore and save
   427	          # an empty string to prevent re-evaluation
   428	          ""
   429	        end
   430	    end
   431	
   432	    if persisted?
   433	      self.update_column(:dominant_color, color)
   434	    else
   435	      self.dominant_color = color
   436	    end
   437	  end
   438	
   439	  def target_image_quality(local_path, test_quality)
   440	    @file_quality ||=
   441	      begin
   442	        Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
   443	          "identify",
   444	          "-ping",
   445	          "-format",
   446	          "%Q",
   447	          local_path,
   448	          timeout: MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
   449	        ).to_i
   450	      rescue StandardError
   451	        0
   452	      end
   453	
   454	    test_quality if @file_quality == 0 || @file_quality > test_quality
   455	  end

   457	  def self.sha1_from_short_path(path)

   459	  end

   461	  def self.sha1_from_short_url(url)

   463	  end

   469	  def self.sha1_from_base62_encoded(encoded_sha1)
   470	    return nil if encoded_sha1.length > MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH
   471	    sha1 = Base62.decode(encoded_sha1).to_s(16)
   472	
   473	    if sha1.length > SHA1_LENGTH
   474	      nil
   475	    else
   476	      sha1.rjust(SHA1_LENGTH, "0")
   477	    end
   478	  end

   492	  def update_secure_status(source: "unknown", override: nil)
   493	    if override.nil?
   494	      mark_secure, reason = UploadSecurity.new(self).should_be_secure_with_reason
   495	    else
   496	      mark_secure = override
   497	      reason = "manually overridden"
   498	    end
   499	
   500	    secure_status_did_change = self.secure? != mark_secure
   501	    self.update(secure_params(mark_secure, reason, source))
   502	
   503	    if secure_status_did_change && Discourse.store.external?
   504	      Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(self)
   505	      sync_optimized_videos_secure_status(mark_secure)
   506	    end
   507	
   508	    secure_status_did_change
   509	  end

   511	  def sync_optimized_videos_secure_status(mark_secure)
   512	    optimized_videos
   513	      .includes(:optimized_upload)
   514	      .each do |optimized_video|
   515	        optimized_upload = optimized_video.optimized_upload
   516	        if optimized_upload.secure? != mark_secure
   517	          optimized_upload.update_secure_status(
   518	            source: "sync_with_original_upload",
   519	            override: mark_secure,
   520	          )
   521	        end
   522	      end
   523	  end
   524	
   525	  def secure_params(secure, reason, source = "unknown")
   526	    {
   527	      secure: secure,
   528	      security_last_changed_reason: reason + " | source: #{source}",
   529	      security_last_changed_at: Time.zone.now,
   530	    }
   531	  end
