   533	  def self.migrate_to_new_scheme(limit: nil)
   534	    problems = []
   535	
   536	    DistributedMutex.synchronize("migrate_upload_to_new_scheme") do
   537	      if SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme
   538	        max_file_size_kb = [
   539	          SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
   540	          SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
   541	        ].max.kilobytes
   542	
   543	        local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
   544	        db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
   545	
   546	        scope =
   547	          Upload
   548	            .by_users
   549	            .where("url NOT LIKE '%/original/_X/%' AND url LIKE ?", "%/uploads/#{db}%")
   550	            .order(id: :desc)
   551	
   552	        scope = scope.limit(limit) if limit
   553	
   554	        if scope.count == 0
   555	          SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme = false
   556	          return problems
   557	        end
   558	
   559	        remap_scope = nil
   560	
   561	        scope.each do |upload|
   562	          begin
   563	            # keep track of the url
   564	            previous_url = upload.url.dup
   565	            # where is the file currently stored?
   566	            external = previous_url =~ %r{\A//}
   567	            # download if external
   568	            if external
   569	              url = SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + previous_url
   570	
   571	              begin
   572	                retries ||= 0
   573	
   574	                file =
   575	                  FileHelper.download(
   576	                    url,
   577	                    max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
   578	                    tmp_file_name: "discourse",
   579	                    follow_redirect: true,
   580	                  )
   581	              rescue OpenURI::HTTPError
   582	                retry if (retries += 1) < 1
   583	                next
   584	              end
   585	
   586	              path = file.path
   587	            else
   588	              path = local_store.path_for(upload)
   589	            end
   590	            # compute SHA if missing
   591	            upload.sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(path) if upload.sha1.blank?
   592	
   593	            # store to new location & update the filesize
   594	            File.open(path) do |f|
   595	              upload.url = Discourse.store.store_upload(f, upload)
   596	              upload.filesize = f.size
   597	              upload.save!(validate: false)
   598	            end
   599	            # remap the URLs
   600	            DbHelper.remap(UrlHelper.absolute(previous_url), upload.url) unless external
   601	
   602	            DbHelper.remap(
   603	              previous_url,
   604	              upload.url,
   605	              excluded_tables: %w[
   606	                posts
   607	                post_search_data
   608	                incoming_emails
   609	                notifications
   610	                single_sign_on_records
   611	                stylesheet_cache
   612	                topic_search_data
   613	                users
   614	                user_emails
   615	                draft_sequences
   616	                optimized_images
   617	              ],
   618	            )
   619	
   620	            remap_scope ||=
   621	              begin
   622	                Post
   623	                  .with_deleted
   624	                  .where(
   625	                    "raw ~ '/uploads/#{db}/\\d+/' OR raw ~ '/uploads/#{db}/original/(\\d|[a-z])/'",
   626	                  )
   627	                  .select(:id, :raw, :cooked)
   628	                  .all
   629	              end
   630	
   631	            remap_scope.each do |post|
   632	              post.raw.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)
   633	              post.cooked.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)
   634	              if post.changed?
   635	                Post.with_deleted.where(id: post.id).update_all(raw: post.raw, cooked: post.cooked)
   636	              end
   637	            end
   638	
   639	            upload.optimized_images.find_each(&:destroy!)
   640	            upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
   641	            # remove the old file (when local)
   642	            FileUtils.rm(path, force: true) unless external
   643	          rescue => e
   644	            problems << { upload: upload, ex: e }
   645	          ensure
   646	            file&.unlink
   647	            file&.close
   648	          end
   649	        end
   650	      end
   651	    end
   652	
   653	    problems
   654	  end
   655	
   656	  def self.extract_upload_ids(raw)
   657	    return [] if raw.blank?
   658	
   659	    sha1s = []
   660	
   661	    raw.scan(/\/(\h{40})/).each { |match| sha1s << match[0] }
   662	
   663	    raw
   664	      .scan(%r{/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)})
   665	      .each { |match| sha1s << Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(match[0]) }
   666	
   667	    Upload.where(sha1: sha1s.uniq).pluck(:id)
   668	  end
   669	
   670	  def self.backfill_dominant_colors!(count)
   671	    Upload
   672	      .where(dominant_color: nil)
   673	      .order("id desc")
   674	      .first(count)
   675	      .each { |upload| upload.calculate_dominant_color! }
   676	  end

   681	    "#{Upload.base62_sha1(sha1)}#{extension.present? ? ".#{extension}" : ""}"

   685	    !skip_video_conversion && SiteSetting.video_conversion_enabled &&
   686	      SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_uploads && FileHelper.is_supported_video?(original_filename) &&
   687	      !OptimizedVideo.exists?(upload_id: id)
   688	  end

Post rendering has multiple touch points: raw upload URLs become `upload://` or `/uploads/short-url`, cooking expands them back through `PrettyText::Helpers`, and post-processing generates thumbnails/lightboxes and records `UploadReference`. That means any Upload URL or identity change has to preserve both raw and cooked representations, not just model associations.

    74	    return nil unless original = image_upload
    75	    return nil if original.filesize >= SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.to_i.kilobytes
    76	    return nil unless original.read_attribute(:width) && original.read_attribute(:height)
    77

    79	    infos << { # Always add original

    81	      max_height: nil,
    82	      width: original.width,
    83	      height: original.height,
    84	      url: original.url,

    88	
    89	    records.each do |record|
    90	      next unless record.optimized_image # Only serialize successful thumbnails
    91	
    92	      infos << {
    93	        max_width: record.max_width,
    94	        max_height: record.max_height,
    95	        width: record.optimized_image&.width,
    96	        height: record.optimized_image&.height,
    97	        url: record.optimized_image&.url,
    98	      }
    99	    end
   100

   102	    if SiteSetting.create_thumbnails && enqueue_if_missing &&
   103	         records.length < thumbnail_sizes.length &&
   104	         Discourse.redis.set(thumbnail_job_redis_key(extra_sizes), 1, nx: true, ex: 1.minute)
   105	      Jobs.enqueue(:generate_topic_thumbnails, { topic_id: id, extra_sizes: extra_sizes })
   106	    end
   107	
   108	    infos.each { |i| i[:url] = UrlHelper.cook_url(i[:url], secure: original.secure?, local: true) }
   109	
   110	    infos.sort_by! { |i| -i[:width] * i[:height] }
   111	  end

   113	  def generate_thumbnails!(extra_sizes: [])
   114	    return nil unless SiteSetting.create_thumbnails
   115	    return nil unless original = image_upload
   116	    return nil if original.filesize >= SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes
   117	    return nil unless original.width && original.height
   118	    extra_sizes = [] unless extra_sizes.kind_of?(Array)
   119	
   120	    (Topic.thumbnail_sizes + extra_sizes).each do |dim|

   122	    end
   123	  end

   126	    thumbnail =

   129	          record.max_height == Topic.share_thumbnail_size[1]
   130	      end
   131	
   132	    if thumbnail.nil? && image_upload && SiteSetting.create_thumbnails &&
   133	         image_upload.filesize < SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes &&
   134	         image_upload.read_attribute(:width) && image_upload.read_attribute(:height) &&
   135	         enqueue_if_missing &&
   136	         Discourse.redis.set(thumbnail_job_redis_key([]), 1, nx: true, ex: 1.minute)
   137	      Jobs.enqueue(:generate_topic_thumbnails, { topic_id: id })
   138	    end
   139	
   140	    raw_url = thumbnail&.optimized_image&.url || image_upload&.url
   141	    UrlHelper.cook_url(raw_url, secure: image_upload&.secure?, local: true) if raw_url
   142	  end

     3	# This model indicates an 'attempt' to create a topic thumbnail
     4	# for an upload. This means we don't keep trying to create optimized
     5	# images for small/invalid original images.
     6	#
     7	# Foreign keys with ON DELETE CASCADE are used to ensure unneeded data
     8	# is deleted automatically
     9	class TopicThumbnail < ActiveRecord::Base
    10	  belongs_to :upload
    11	  belongs_to :optimized_image
    12	
    13	  def self.find_or_create_for!(original, max_width:, max_height:)
    14	    existing =
    15	      TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
    16	    return existing if existing
    17	    return nil if !SiteSetting.create_thumbnails?
    18	
    19	    target_width, target_height =
    20	      ImageSizer.resize(
    21	        original.width,
    22	        original.height,
    23	        { max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height },
    24	      )
    25	
    26	    needs_optimization = target_width < original.width && target_height < original.height
    27	    skip_animated = SiteSetting.animated_topic_thumbnails && original.animated?
    28	
    29	    if needs_optimization && !skip_animated
    30	      optimized = OptimizedImage.create_for(original, target_width, target_height)
    31	    end
    32	
    33	    # may have been associated already, bulk insert will skip dupes
    34	    TopicThumbnail.insert_all(
    35	      [
    36	        upload_id: original.id,
    37	        max_width: max_width,
    38	        max_height: max_height,
    39	        optimized_image_id: optimized&.id,
    40	      ],
    41	    )
    42	
    43	    TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
    44	  end

    46	  def self.ensure_consistency!
    47	    # Clean up records for broken upload links or broken optimized image links
    48	    TopicThumbnail
    49	      .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads on upload_id = uploads.id")
    50	      .joins("LEFT JOIN optimized_images on optimized_image_id = optimized_images.id")
    51	      .where(<<~SQL)
    52	        (optimized_image_id IS NOT NULL AND optimized_images IS NULL)
    53	        OR uploads IS NULL
    54	      SQL
    55	      .delete_all
    56	
    57	    # Delete records for sizes which are no longer needed
    58	    sizes =
    59	      Topic.thumbnail_sizes +
    60	        ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
    61	    sizes_sql =
    62	      sizes.map { |s| "(max_width = #{s[0].to_i} AND max_height = #{s[1].to_i})" }.join(" OR ")
    63	    TopicThumbnail.where.not(sizes_sql).delete_all
    64	  end

We are not connected to the net, or SSRF checks failed. 150 ensure 151 tempfile.close! if tempfile && tempfile.respond_to?(:close!) 152 end 153 154 def self.ensure_consistency!(max_optimized_avatars_to_remove: 20_000) 155 DB.exec <<~SQL 156 DELETE FROM user_avatars 157 USING user_avatars ua 158 LEFT JOIN users u ON ua.user_id = u.id 159 WHERE user_avatars.id = ua.id AND u.id IS NULL 160 SQL 161 162 DB.exec <<~SQL 163 UPDATE user_avatars 164 SET gravatar_upload_id = NULL 165 WHERE gravatar_upload_id IN ( 166 SELECT u1.gravatar_upload_id FROM user_avatars u1 167 LEFT JOIN uploads up 168 ON u1.gravatar_upload_id = up.id 169 WHERE u1.gravatar_upload_id IS NOT NULL AND 170 up.id IS NULL 171 ) 172 SQL 173 174 DB.exec <<~SQL 175 UPDATE user_avatars 176 SET custom_upload_id = NULL 177 WHERE custom_upload_id IN ( 178 SELECT u1.custom_upload_id FROM user_avatars u1 179 LEFT JOIN uploads up 180 ON u1.custom_upload_id = up.id 181 WHERE u1.custom_upload_id IS NOT NULL AND 182 up.id IS NULL 183 ) 184 SQL 185 186 ids = 187 DB.query_single(<<~SQL, sizes: Discourse.avatar_sizes, limit: max_optimized_avatars_to_remove) 188 SELECT oi.id FROM ( 189 SELECT custom_upload_id FROM user_avatars 190 EXCEPT 191 SELECT upload_id FROM upload_references WHERE target_type <> 'UserAvatar' 192 AND upload_id IS NOT NULL 193 ) AS a 194 JOIN optimized_images oi ON oi.upload_id = a.custom_upload_id 195 WHERE oi.width not in (:sizes) AND oi.height not in (:sizes) 196 LIMIT :limit 197 SQL 198 199 warnings_reported = 0 200 1 # frozen_string_literal: true 2 3 class UserProfile < ActiveRecord::Base 4 BAKED_VERSION = 1 5 6 belongs_to :user, inverse_of: :user_profile 7 belongs_to :card_background_upload, class_name: "Upload" 8 belongs_to :profile_background_upload, class_name: "Upload" 9 belongs_to :granted_title_badge, class_name: "Badge" 10 belongs_to :featured_topic, class_name: "Topic" 11 12 has_many :upload_references, as: :target, dependent: :destroy 13 has_many :user_profile_views, dependent: :destroy 14 15 validates :bio_raw, length: { maximum: 3000 }, watched_words: true 16 validates :website, 17 url: true, 18 length: { 19 maximum: 3000, 20 }, 21 allow_blank: true, 22 if: :validate_website? 23 validates :location, length: { maximum: 3000 }, watched_words: true 24 validates :user, presence: true 25 26 validate :website_domain_validator, if: :validate_website? 27 28 before_save :cook 29 before_save :apply_watched_words, if: :location? 30 31 after_save :trigger_badges 32 after_save :pull_hotlinked_image 33 34 after_save do 35 if saved_change_to_profile_background_upload_id? || 36 saved_change_to_card_background_upload_id? || saved_change_to_bio_raw? 37 upload_ids = 38 [self.profile_background_upload_id, self.card_background_upload_id] + 39 Upload.extract_upload_ids(self.bio_raw) 40 UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self) 41 end 42 end 43 44 attr_accessor :skip_pull_hotlinked_image 45 46 def bio_excerpt(length = 350, opts = {}) 47 return nil if bio_cooked.blank? 48 excerpt = PrettyText.excerpt(bio_cooked, length, opts).sub(/
\z/, "") 49 return excerpt if excerpt.blank? || (user.has_trust_level?(TrustLevel[1]) && !user.suspended?) 50 PrettyText.strip_links(excerpt) 51 end 52 53 def bio_processed 54 if bio_cooked.blank? || (user.has_trust_level?(TrustLevel[1]) && !user.suspended?) 55 return bio_cooked 56 end 57 PrettyText.strip_links(bio_cooked) 58 end 59 60 def bio_summary 61 bio_excerpt(500, strip_links: true, text_entities: true) 62 end 63 64 def recook_bio 65 self.bio_raw_will_change! 66 cook 67 end 68 69 def upload_card_background(upload) 70 self.update!(card_background_upload: upload) 90 unless SiteSetting.enable_discourse_connect && SiteSetting.discourse_connect_overrides_location 91 user_profile.location = attributes.fetch(:location) { user_profile.location } 92 end 93 94 unless SiteSetting.enable_discourse_connect && SiteSetting.discourse_connect_overrides_website 95 user_profile.website = format_url(attributes.fetch(:website) { user_profile.website }) 96 end 97 98 if attributes[:profile_background_upload_url] == "" || 99 !guardian.can_upload_profile_header?(user) 100 user_profile.profile_background_upload_id = nil 101 elsif upload = Upload.get_from_url(attributes[:profile_background_upload_url]) 102 user_profile.profile_background_upload_id = upload.id 103 end 104 105 if attributes[:card_background_upload_url] == "" || 106 !guardian.can_upload_user_card_background?(user) 107 user_profile.card_background_upload_id = nil 108 elsif upload = Upload.get_from_url(attributes[:card_background_upload_url]) 109 user_profile.card_background_upload_id = upload.id 110 end 111 112 if attributes[:user_notification_schedule]

Direct model dependents include posts/topics, user avatars/profile backgrounds, badges, categories, group flair, theme fields/settings, site settings, exports, emoji, chat messages, calendar events, and AI RAG/tool uploads. I’m pulling the storage, job, backup/restore, and plugin edges next because those are the places most likely to assume physical paths and cleanup behavior.

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module BackupRestore
     4	  UploadsRestoreError = Class.new(RuntimeError)
     5	
     6	  class UploadsRestorer
     7	    delegate :log, to: :@logger, private: true
     8	
     9	    S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX = /\.s3(?:\.dualstack\.[a-z0-9\-]+?|[.\-][a-z0-9\-]+?)?\.amazonaws\.com/
    10	
    11	    def self.s3_regex_string(s3_base_url)
    12	      clean_url = s3_base_url.sub(S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX, ".s3.amazonaws.com")
    13	
    14	      regex_string =
    15	        clean_url
    16	          .split(".s3.amazonaws.com")
    17	          .map { |s| Regexp.escape(s) }
    18	          .insert(1, S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX.source)
    19	          .join("")
    20	
    21	      [regex_string, clean_url]
    22	    end
    23	
    24	    def initialize(logger)
    25	      @logger = logger
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    def restore(tmp_directory)
    29	      upload_directories =
    30	        Dir
    31	          .glob(File.join(tmp_directory, "uploads", "*"))
    32	          .reject { |path| File.basename(path).start_with?("PaxHeaders") }
    33	
    34	      if upload_directories.count > 1
    35	        raise UploadsRestoreError.new(
    36	                "Could not find uploads, because the uploads " \
    37	                  "directory contains multiple folders.",
    38	              )
    39	      end
    40	
    41	      @tmp_uploads_path = upload_directories.first
    42	      return if @tmp_uploads_path.blank?
    43	
    44	      @previous_db_name = BackupMetadata.value_for("db_name") || File.basename(@tmp_uploads_path)
    45	      @current_db_name = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
    46	      backup_contains_optimized_images = File.exist?(File.join(@tmp_uploads_path, "optimized"))
    47	
    48	      remap_uploads
    49	      restore_uploads
    50	
    51	      generate_optimized_images unless backup_contains_optimized_images
    52	      rebake_posts_with_uploads
    53	    end
    54	
    55	    protected
    56	
    57	    def restore_uploads
    58	      store = Discourse.store
    59	
    60	      if !store.respond_to?(:copy_from)
    61	        # a FileStore implementation from a plugin might not support this method, so raise a helpful error
    62	        store_name = Discourse.store.class.name
    63	        raise UploadsRestoreError.new(
    64	                "The current file store (#{store_name}) does not support restoring uploads.",
    65	              )
    66	      end
    67	
    68	      log "Restoring uploads, this may take a while..."
    69	      store.copy_from(@tmp_uploads_path)
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    # Remaps upload URLs depending on old and new configuration.
    73	    # URLs of uploads differ a little bit between local uploads and uploads stored on S3.
    74	    # Multisites are another reason why URLs can be different.
    75	    #
    76	    # Examples:
    77	    #   * regular site, local storage
    78	    #     /uploads/default/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
    79	    #
    80	    #   * regular site, S3
    81	    #     //bucket-name.s3.dualstack.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
    82	    #
    83	    #   * multisite, local storage
    84	    #     /uploads/<site-name>/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
    85	    #
    86	    #   * multisite, S3
    87	    #     //bucket-name.s3.dualstack.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/uploads/<site-name>/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
    88	    def remap_uploads
    89	      log "Remapping uploads..."
    90	
    91	      was_multisite = BackupMetadata.value_for("multisite") == "t"
    92	      upload_path = "/#{Discourse.store.upload_path}/"
    93	      uploads_folder = was_multisite ? "/" : upload_path
    94	
    95	      if (old_base_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("base_url")) && old_base_url != Discourse.base_url
    96	        remap(old_base_url, Discourse.base_url)
    97	      end
    98	
    99	      current_s3_base_url =
   100	        SiteSetting::Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting::Upload.s3_base_url : nil
   101	      if (old_s3_base_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("s3_base_url")) &&
   102	           old_s3_base_url != current_s3_base_url
   103	        remap_s3("#{old_s3_base_url}/", uploads_folder)
   104	      end
   105	
   106	      current_s3_cdn_url =
   107	        SiteSetting::Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting::Upload.s3_cdn_url : nil
   108	      if (old_s3_cdn_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("s3_cdn_url")) &&
   109	           old_s3_cdn_url != current_s3_cdn_url
   110	        base_url = current_s3_cdn_url || Discourse.base_url
   111	        remap("#{old_s3_cdn_url}/", UrlHelper.schemaless("#{base_url}#{uploads_folder}"))
   112	
   113	        old_host = URI.parse(old_s3_cdn_url).host
   114	        new_host = URI.parse(base_url).host
   115	        remap(old_host, new_host) if old_host != new_host
   116	      end
   117	
   118	      if (old_cdn_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("cdn_url")) && old_cdn_url != Discourse.asset_host
   119	        base_url = Discourse.asset_host || Discourse.base_url
   120	        remap("#{old_cdn_url}/", UrlHelper.schemaless("#{base_url}/"))
   121	
   122	        old_host = URI.parse(old_cdn_url).host
   123	        new_host = URI.parse(base_url).host
   124	        remap(old_host, new_host) if old_host != new_host
   125	      end
   126	
   127	      remap("/uploads/#{@previous_db_name}/", upload_path) if @previous_db_name != @current_db_name
   128	    rescue => ex
   129	      log "Something went wrong while remapping uploads.", ex
   130	    end
   131	
   132	    def remap(from, to)
   133	      log "Remapping '#{from}' to '#{to}'"
   134	      DbHelper.remap(from, to, verbose: true, excluded_tables: ["backup_metadata"])
   135	    end
   136	
   137	    def remap_s3(old_s3_base_url, uploads_folder)
   138	      if old_s3_base_url.include?("amazonaws.com")
   139	        from_regex, from_clean_url = self.class.s3_regex_string(old_s3_base_url)
   140	        log "Remapping with regex from '#{from_clean_url}' to '#{uploads_folder}'"
   141	        DbHelper.regexp_replace(
   142	          from_regex,
   143	          uploads_folder,
   144	          verbose: true,
   145	          excluded_tables: ["backup_metadata"],
   146	        )
   147	      else
   148	        remap(old_s3_base_url, uploads_folder)
   149	      end
   150	    end
   151	
   152	    def generate_optimized_images
   153	      log "Optimizing site icons..."
   154	      DB.exec("TRUNCATE TABLE optimized_images")
   155	      SiteIconManager.ensure_optimized!
   156	
   157	      User
   158	        .where.not(uploaded_avatar_id: nil)
   159	        .find_each do |user|
   160	          Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, upload_id: user.uploaded_avatar_id)
   161	        end
   162	    end
   163	
   164	    def rebake_posts_with_uploads
   165	      log "Posts will be rebaked by a background job in sidekiq. You will see missing images until that has completed."
   166	      log 'You can expedite the process by manually running "rake posts:rebake_uncooked_posts"'
   167	
   168	      DB.exec(<<~SQL)
   169	        UPDATE posts
   170	        SET baked_version = NULL
   171	        WHERE id IN (SELECT target_id FROM upload_references WHERE target_type = 'Post')
   172	      SQL
   173	    end
   174	  end
   175	end

     5	module BackupRestore
     6	  RestoreDisabledError = Class.new(RuntimeError)
     7	  FilenameMissingError = Class.new(RuntimeError)
     8	
     9	  class Restorer
    10	    delegate :log, to: :@logger, private: true
    11	
    12	    attr_reader :success
    13	
    14	    def initialize(
    15	      user_id:,
    16	      filename:,
    17	      factory:,
    18	      disable_emails: true,
    19	      location: nil,
    20	      interactive: false
    21	    )
    22	      @user_id = user_id
    23	      @filename = filename
    24	      @factory = factory
    25	      @logger = factory.logger
    26	      @disable_emails = disable_emails
    27	      @interactive = interactive
    28	
    29	      ensure_restore_is_enabled
    30	      ensure_we_have_a_user
    31	      ensure_we_have_a_filename
    32	
    33	      @success = false
    34	      @current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
    35	
    36	      @system = factory.create_system_interface
    37	      @backup_file_handler = factory.create_backup_file_handler(@filename, @current_db, location)
    38	      @database_restorer = factory.create_database_restorer(@current_db)
    39	      @uploads_restorer = factory.create_uploads_restorer
    40	    end
    41	
    42	    def run
    43	      log "[STARTED]"
    44	      log "'#{@user_info[:username]}' has started the restore!"
    45	
    46	      # FIXME not atomic!
    47	      ensure_no_operation_is_running
    48	      @system.mark_restore_as_running
    49	
    50	      @system.listen_for_shutdown_signal
    51	
    52	      @filename, @tmp_directory, db_dump_path = @backup_file_handler.decompress
    53	      validate_backup_metadata
    54	
    55	      @system.enable_readonly_mode
    56	      @system.pause_sidekiq("restore")
    57	      @system.wait_for_sidekiq
    58	      @system.flush_redis
    59	      @system.clear_sidekiq_queues
    60	
    61	      @database_restorer.restore(db_dump_path, @interactive)
    62	
    63	      reload_site_settings
    64	      run_seed_fu
    65	
    66	      @system.disable_readonly_mode
    67	
    68	      clear_category_cache
    69	      clear_stats
    70	      reload_translations
    71	
    72	      restore_uploads
    73	
    74	      clear_emoji_cache
    75	      clear_theme_cache
    76	
    77	      after_restore_hook
    78	    rescue Compression::Strategy::ExtractFailed
    79	      log "ERROR: The uncompressed file is too big. Consider increasing the hidden " \
    80	            '"decompressed_backup_max_file_size_mb" setting.'
    81	      @database_restorer.rollback
    82	    rescue SystemExit
    83	      log "Restore process was cancelled!"
    84	      @database_restorer.rollback
    85	    rescue => ex
    86	      log "EXCEPTION: " + ex.message
    87	      log ex.backtrace.join("\n")
    88	      @database_restorer.rollback
    89	    else
    90	      @success = true
    91	    ensure
    92	      clean_up
    93	      notify_user
    94	      log "Finished!"
    95	
    96	      @success ? log("[SUCCESS]") : log("[FAILED]")
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    protected
   100	
   101	    def ensure_restore_is_enabled
   102	      return if Rails.env.development? || SiteSetting.allow_restore?
   103	      raise BackupRestore::RestoreDisabledError
   104	    end
   105	
   106	    def ensure_no_operation_is_running
   107	      raise BackupRestore::OperationRunningError if BackupRestore.is_operation_running?
   108	    end
   109	
   110	    def ensure_we_have_a_user
   111	      user = User.find_by(id: @user_id)
   112	      raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:user_id) if user.blank?
   113	
   114	      # keep some user data around to check them against the newly restored database
   115	      @user_info = { id: user.id, username: user.username, email: user.email }
   116	    end
   117	
   118	    def ensure_we_have_a_filename
   119	      raise BackupRestore::FilenameMissingError if @filename.nil?
   120	    end
   121	
   122	    def validate_backup_metadata
   123	      @factory.create_meta_data_handler(@filename, @tmp_directory).validate
   124	    end
   125	
   126	    def reload_site_settings
   127	      log "Reloading site settings..."
   128	      SiteSetting.refresh!
   129	
   130	      DiscourseEvent.trigger(:site_settings_restored)
   131	
   132	      if @disable_emails && SiteSetting.disable_emails == "no"
   133	        log "Disabling outgoing emails for non-staff users..."
   134	        user = User.find_by_email(@user_info[:email]) || Discourse.system_user
   135	        SiteSetting.set_and_log(:disable_emails, "non-staff", user)
   136	      end
   137	    end
   138	
   139	    def run_seed_fu
   140	      log "Running seed fu..."
   141	
   142	      begin
   143	        Discourse::Application.load_tasks
   144	        Rake::Task["db:seed"].invoke
   145	      rescue => ex
   146	        log "Failed to run seed fu", ex
   147	      end
   148	    end
   149	
   150	    def clear_category_cache
   151	      log "Clearing category cache..."
   152	      Category.reset_topic_ids_cache
   153	      Category.clear_subcategory_ids
   154	    end
   155	
   156	    def clear_emoji_cache
   157	      log "Clearing emoji cache..."
   158	      Emoji.clear_cache
   159	    rescue => ex
   160	      log "Something went wrong while clearing emoji cache.", ex
   161	    end
   162	
   163	    def reload_translations
   164	      log "Reloading translations..."
   165	      TranslationOverride.reload_all_overrides!
   166	    end
   167	
   168	    def restore_uploads
   169	      if @interactive
   170	        puts ""
   171	        puts "Attention! Pausing restore before uploads.".red.bold
   172	        puts "You can work on the restored database in a separate Rails console."
   173	        puts ""
   174	        puts "Press any key to continue with the restore.".bold
   175	        puts ""
   176	        STDIN.getch
   177	      end
   178	
   179	      @uploads_restorer.restore(@tmp_directory)
   180	    end
   181	
   182	    def notify_user
   183	      return if @success && @user_id == Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID
   184	
   185	      if user = User.find_by_email(@user_info[:email])
   186	        log "Notifying '#{user.username}' of the end of the restore..."
   187	        status = @success ? :restore_succeeded : :restore_failed
   188	
   189	        logs = Discourse::Utils.logs_markdown(@logger.logs, user: user)
   190	        SystemMessage.create_from_system_user(user, status, logs: logs)
   191	      else
   192	        log "Could not send notification to '#{@user_info[:username]}' " \
   193	              "(#{@user_info[:email]}), because the user does not exist."
   194	      end
   195	    rescue => ex
   196	      log "Something went wrong while notifying user.", ex
   197	    end
   198	
   199	    def clean_up
   200	      log "Cleaning stuff up..."
   201	      @database_restorer.clean_up
   202	      @backup_file_handler.clean_up
   203	      @system.unpause_sidekiq
   204	      @system.disable_readonly_mode if Discourse.readonly_mode?
   205	      @system.mark_restore_as_not_running
   206	    end
   207	
   208	    def clear_theme_cache
   209	      log "Clear theme cache"
   210	      ThemeField.force_recompilation!
   211	      Theme.expire_site_cache!
   212	      Stylesheet::Manager.cache.clear
   213	    rescue => ex
   214	      log "Something went wrong while clearing theme cache.", ex
   215	    end
   216	
   217	    def clear_stats
   218	      Discourse.stats.remove("missing_s3_uploads")
   219	    end
   220

108 email_log.cc_addresses = cc_addresses.join(";") 109 email_log.cc_user_ids = User.with_email(cc_addresses).pluck(:id) 110 end 360 def digest_posts 361 Post.where(id: header_value("X-Discourse-Post-Ids")&.split(",")) 362 end 363 364 def add_attachments(*posts, is_digest: false) 365 max_email_size = SiteSetting.email_total_attachment_size_limit_kb.kilobytes 366 return if max_email_size == 0 367 368 email_size = 0 369 posts.each do |post| 370 next unless DiscoursePluginRegistry.apply_modifier(:should_add_email_attachments, post) 371 372 post.uploads.each do |original_upload| 373 optimized_1X = original_upload.optimized_images.first 374 375 if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(original_upload.original_filename) 376 next if !should_attach_image?(original_upload, optimized_1X) 377 # Don't attach images that aren't rendered in the e-mail. 378 next if is_digest && !@message.html_part.body.include?(original_upload.sha1) 379 else 380 # Only attach images in digests. 381 next if is_digest 382 end 383 384 attached_upload = optimized_1X || original_upload 385 next if email_size + attached_upload.filesize > max_email_size 386 387 begin 388 path = 389 if attached_upload.local? 390 Discourse.store.path_for(attached_upload) 391 else 392 Discourse.store.download!(attached_upload) 393 end 394 395 @message_attachments_index[original_upload.sha1] = @message.attachments.size 396 @message.attachments[original_upload.original_filename] = File.read(path) 397 email_size += File.size(path) 398 rescue => e 399 Discourse.warn_exception( 400 e, 401 message: "Failed to attach file to email", 402 env: { 403 post_id: post.id, 404 upload_id: original_upload.id, 405 filename: original_upload.original_filename, 406 }, 407 ) 408 end 409 end 410 end 411 412 fix_parts_after_attachments! 413 end 414 415 def should_attach_image?(upload, optimized_1X = nil) 416 if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_allow_embed_images_in_emails || 417 # Sometimes images in a post have a secure URL but are not secure uploads, 418 # for example if a user uploads an image to a public post then copies the markdown 419 # into a PM which sends an email, so we have to make sure we attached those 420 # stripped images here as well. 421 ( 422 !upload.secure? && !@stripped_secure_upload_shas.include?(upload.sha1) && 423 !@stripped_secure_upload_shas.include?(optimized_1X&.sha1) 424 ) 425 return 426 end 427 if (optimized_1X&.filesize || upload.filesize) > 428 SiteSetting.secure_uploads_max_email_embed_image_size_kb.kilobytes 429 return 430 end 431 true 432 end 433 434 # 435 # Two behaviors in the mail gem collide: 1368 add_elided_to_raw!(options) 1369 options[:raw] = add_attachments(options[:raw], options[:user], options) 1370 create_post(options) 1371 end 1372 1373 def add_attachments(raw, user, options = {}) 1374 raw = raw.dup 1375 1376 upload_ids = 1377 UploadReference.where( 1378 target_id: Post.where(topic_id: options[:topic_id]).select(:id), 1379 ).pluck("DISTINCT upload_id") 1380 1381 upload_shas = Upload.where(id: upload_ids).pluck("DISTINCT COALESCE(original_sha1, sha1)") 1382 1383 is_duplicate = ->(upload_id, upload_sha, attachment) do 1384 return true if upload_id && upload_ids.include?(upload_id) 1385 return true if upload_sha && upload_shas.include?(upload_sha) 1386 1387 if attachment.respond_to?(:url) && attachment.url&.start_with?("cid:") && 1388 attachment.content_type&.start_with?("image/") 1389 return true if @cids&.include?(attachment.url) 1390 end 1391 1392 false 1393 end 1394 1395 added_attachments = [] 1396 rejected_attachments = [] 1397 1398 attachments.each do |attachment| 1399 tmp = Tempfile.new(["discourse-email-attachment", File.extname(attachment.filename)]) 1400 begin 1401 # read attachment 1402 File.open(tmp.path, "w+b") { |f| f.write attachment.body.decoded } 1403 # create the upload for the user 1404 opts = { for_group_message: options[:is_group_message] } 1405 upload = UploadCreator.new(tmp, attachment.filename, opts).create_for(user.id) 1406 upload_sha = upload.original_sha1.presence || upload.sha1 1407 if upload.errors.empty? 1408 # try to inline images 1409 if attachment.content_type&.start_with?("image/") 1410 if raw[attachment.url] 1411 raw.sub!(attachment.url, upload.url) 1412 1413 InlineUploads.match_img( 1414 raw, 1415 uploads: { 1416 upload.url => upload, 1417 }, 1418 ) do |match, src, replacement, _| 1419 raw = raw.sub(match, replacement) if src == upload.url 1420 end 1421 elsif raw[/\[image:[^\]]*\]/i] 1422 raw.sub!(/\[image:[^\]]*\]/i, UploadMarkdown.new(upload).to_markdown) 1423 elsif !is_duplicate[upload.id, upload_sha, attachment] 1424 added_attachments << upload 1425 end 1426 elsif !is_duplicate[upload.id, upload_sha, attachment] 1427 added_attachments << upload 1428 end 1429 else 1430 rejected_attachments << upload 1431 raw << "\n\n#{I18n.t("emails.incoming.missing_attachment", filename: upload.original_filename)}\n\n" 1432 end 1433 ensure 1434 tmp&.close! 1435 end 1436 end 1437 1438 if rejected_attachments.present? && !user.staged? 1439 notify_about_rejected_attachment(rejected_attachments) 1440 end 1441 1442 if added_attachments.present? 1443 markdown = 1444 added_attachments.map { |upload| UploadMarkdown.new(upload).to_markdown }.join("\n") 1445 if markdown.present? 1446 raw << "\n\n" 1447 raw << "[details=\"#{I18n.t("emails.incoming.attachments")}\"]" 1448 raw << "\n\n" 1449 raw << markdown 1450 raw << "\n\n" 360 361 def stripped_upload_sha_map 362 @stripped_upload_sha_map ||= 363 begin 364 upload_shas = {} 365 stripped_media.each do |div| 366 url = div["data-stripped-secure-media"] || div["data-stripped-secure-upload"] 367 upload_shas[url] = Upload.sha1_from_long_url(url) 368 end 369 upload_shas 370 end 371 end 372 373 def stripped_secure_image_uploads 374 upload_shas = stripped_upload_sha_map 375 Upload.select(:original_filename, :sha1).where(sha1: upload_shas.values) 376 end 377 378 def inline_secure_images(attachments, attachments_index) 379 uploads = stripped_secure_image_uploads 380 upload_shas = stripped_upload_sha_map 381 stripped_media.each do |div| 382 upload = 383 uploads.find do |upl| 384 upl.sha1 == 385 upload_shas[div["data-stripped-secure-media"] || div["data-stripped-secure-upload"]] 386 end 387 next if !upload 388 389 if attachments[attachments_index[upload.sha1]] 390 url = attachments[attachments_index[upload.sha1]].url 535 end 536 537 def replace_secure_uploads_urls 538 # strip again, this can be done at a lower level like in the user 539 # notification template but that may not catch everything 540 PrettyText.strip_secure_uploads(@fragment) 541 542 style( 543 "div.secure-upload-notice", 544 "border: 5px solid #e9e9e9; padding: 5px; display: inline-block;", 545 )

     6	  class ProcessPost < ::Jobs::Base
     7	    def execute(args)
     8	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("process_post_#{args[:post_id]}", validity: 10.minutes) do
     9	        post = Post.find_by(id: args[:post_id])
    10	        # two levels of deletion
    11	        return if post.blank? || post.topic.blank?
    12	
    13	        orig_cooked = post.cooked
    14	        recooked = nil
    15	
    16	        if args[:cook].present?
    17	          cooking_options = args[:cooking_options] || {}
    18	          cooking_options[:topic_id] = post.topic_id
    19	          recooked = post.cook(post.raw, cooking_options.symbolize_keys)
    20	          post.update_columns(
    21	            cooked: recooked,
    22	            baked_at: Time.zone.now,
    23	            baked_version: Post::BAKED_VERSION,
    24	          )
    25	        end
    26	
    27	        cp = CookedPostProcessor.new(post, args)
    28	        cp.post_process(new_post: args[:new_post])
    29	
    30	        # If we changed the document, save it
    31	        cooked = cp.html
    32	
    33	        if cooked != (recooked || orig_cooked)
    34	          if orig_cooked.present? && cooked.blank?
    35	            # TODO stop/restart the worker if needed, let's gather a few here first
    36	            Rails.logger.warn(
    37	              "Cooked post processor in FATAL state, bypassing. You need to urgently restart sidekiq\norig: #{orig_cooked}\nrecooked: #{recooked}\ncooked: #{cooked}\npost id: #{post.id}",
    38	            )
    39	          else
    40	            post.update_column(:cooked, cp.html)
    41	            post.topic.update_excerpt(post.excerpt_for_topic) if post.is_first_post?
    42	            extract_links(post)
    43	            post.publish_change_to_clients! :revised
    44	          end
    45	        end
    46	
    47	        enqueue_pull_hotlinked_images(post) unless args[:skip_pull_hotlinked_images]
    48	
    49	        if !post.user&.staff? && !post.user&.staged?
    50	          s = post.raw
    51	          s << " #{post.topic.title}" if post.post_number == 1
    52	          word_watcher = WordWatcher.new(s)
    53	          if !args[:bypass_bump] && word_watcher.should_flag?
    54	            words = word_watcher.word_matches_for_action?(:flag, all_matches: true)
    55	            PostActionCreator.create(
    56	              Discourse.system_user,
    57	              post,
    58	              :inappropriate,
    59	              reason: :watched_word,
    60	              context: words.join(","),

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	require "aws-sdk-s3"
     4	require "csv"
     5	
     6	class S3Inventory
     7	  attr_reader :type, :inventory_date, :s3_helper
     8	
     9	  CSV_KEY_INDEX = 1
    10	  CSV_ETAG_INDEX = 2
    11	  INVENTORY_PREFIX = "inventory"
    12	  INVENTORY_LAG = 2.days
    13	  WAIT_AFTER_RESTORE_DAYS = 2
    14	
    15	  def initialize(
    16	    type,
    17	    s3_inventory_bucket:,
    18	    preloaded_inventory_file: nil,
    19	    preloaded_inventory_date: nil,
    20	    s3_options: {}
    21	  )
    22	    @s3_helper = S3Helper.new(s3_inventory_bucket, "", s3_options)
    23	
    24	    if preloaded_inventory_file && preloaded_inventory_date
    25	      # Data preloaded, so we don't need to fetch it again
    26	      @preloaded_inventory_file = preloaded_inventory_file
    27	      @inventory_date = preloaded_inventory_date
    28	    end
    29	
    30	    if type == :upload
    31	      @type = "original"
    32	      @model = Upload
    33	      @scope = @model.by_users.without_s3_file_missing_confirmed_verification_status
    34	    elsif type == :optimized
    35	      @type = "optimized"
    36	      @scope = @model = OptimizedImage
    37	    else
    38	      raise "Invalid type: #{type}"
    39	    end
    40	  end
    41	
    42	  def backfill_etags_and_list_missing
    43	    if !@preloaded_inventory_file && files.blank?
    44	      error("Failed to list inventory from S3")
    45	      return
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    DistributedMutex.synchronize("s3_inventory_list_missing_#{type}", validity: 30.minutes) do
    49	      begin
    50	        download_and_decompress_files if !@preloaded_inventory_file
    51	
    52	        multisite_prefix = Discourse.store.upload_path
    53	
    54	        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
    55	          begin
    56	            connection.exec(
    57	              "CREATE TEMP TABLE #{tmp_table_name}(url text UNIQUE, etag text, PRIMARY KEY(etag, url))",
    58	            )
    59	
    60	            connection.copy_data("COPY #{tmp_table_name} FROM STDIN CSV") do
    61	              for_each_inventory_row do |row|
    62	                key = row[CSV_KEY_INDEX]
    63	
    64	                next if Rails.configuration.multisite && key.exclude?(multisite_prefix)
    65	                next if key.exclude?("#{type}/")
    66	
    67	                url = File.join(Discourse.store.absolute_base_url, key)
    68	                connection.put_copy_data("#{CGI.unescape(url)},#{row[CSV_ETAG_INDEX]}\n")
    69	              end
    70	            end
    71	
    72	            table_name = @model.table_name
    73	
    74	            # backfilling etags
    75	            connection.async_exec(
    76	              "UPDATE #{table_name}
    77	              SET etag = #{tmp_table_name}.etag
    78	              FROM #{tmp_table_name}
    79	              WHERE #{table_name}.etag IS NULL AND
    80	                #{table_name}.url = #{tmp_table_name}.url",
    81	            )
    82	
    83	            uploads = @scope.where("updated_at < ?", inventory_date)
    84	
    85	            missing_uploads =
    86	              uploads.joins(
    87	                "LEFT JOIN #{tmp_table_name} ON #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag",
    88	              ).where("#{tmp_table_name}.etag IS NULL")
    89	
    90	            exists_with_different_etag =
    91	              missing_uploads
    92	                .joins(
    93	                  "LEFT JOIN #{tmp_table_name} inventory2 ON inventory2.url = #{table_name}.url",
    94	                )
    95	                .where.not(inventory2: { etag: nil })
    96	                .pluck(:id)
    97	
    98	            # marking as verified/not verified
    99	            if @model == Upload
   100	              sql_params = {
   101	                inventory_date: inventory_date,
   102	                invalid_etag: Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag],
   103	                s3_file_missing_confirmed: Upload.verification_statuses[:s3_file_missing_confirmed],
   104	                verified: Upload.verification_statuses[:verified],
   105	                seeded_id_threshold: @model::SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD,
   106	              }
   107	
   108	              DB.exec(<<~SQL, sql_params)
   109	                UPDATE #{table_name}
   110	                SET verification_status = :verified
   111	                WHERE etag IS NOT NULL
   112	                  AND verification_status <> :verified
   113	                  AND verification_status <> :s3_file_missing_confirmed
   114	                  AND updated_at < :inventory_date
   115	                  AND id > :seeded_id_threshold
   116	                  AND EXISTS
   117	                    (
   118	                        SELECT 1
   119	                        FROM #{tmp_table_name}
   120	                        WHERE #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag
   121	                    )
   122	              SQL
   123	
   124	              DB.exec(<<~SQL, sql_params)
   125	                UPDATE #{table_name}
   126	                SET verification_status = :invalid_etag
   127	                WHERE verification_status <> :invalid_etag
   128	                  AND verification_status <> :s3_file_missing_confirmed
   129	                  AND updated_at < :inventory_date
   130	                  AND id > :seeded_id_threshold
   131	                  AND NOT EXISTS
   132	                    (
   133	                        SELECT 1
   134	                        FROM #{tmp_table_name}
   135	                        WHERE #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag
   136	                    )
   137	              SQL
   138	            end
   139	
   140	            if (missing_count = missing_uploads.count) > 0
   141	              missing_uploads
   142	                .select(:id, :url)
   143	                .find_each do |upload|
   144	                  if exists_with_different_etag.include?(upload.id)
   145	                    log "#{upload.url} has different etag"
   146	                  else
   147	                    log upload.url
   148	                  end
   149	                end

  1371	  end
  1372	
  1373	  def hydrate_uploads_in_object(object, properties, uploads_by_id)
  1374	    properties.each do |prop_key, prop_value|
  1375	      case prop_value[:type]
  1376	      when "upload"
  1377	        key = prop_key.to_s
  1378	        upload_id = object[key]
  1379	        upload = uploads_by_id[upload_id]
  1380	        object[key] = upload.url if upload
  1381	      when "objects"
  1382	        nested_objects = object[prop_key.to_s]

     3	class UploadSettingValidator
     4	  def initialize(opts = {})
     5	    @opts = opts
     6	  end
     7	
     8	  def valid_value?(val)
     9	    return true if val.blank?
    10	
    11	    upload = Upload.find_by(id: val)
    12	    upload.present? && additional_validation_passed(upload)
    13	  end
    14	
    15	  def error_message
    16	    return I18n.t("site_settings.errors.invalid_svg") if @opts[:name] == :splash_screen_image
    17	    I18n.t("site_settings.errors.invalid_upload")
    18	  end
    19	
    20	  def additional_validation_passed(upload)
    21	    if @opts[:name] == :splash_screen_image
    22	      validate_svg(upload)
    23	    else
    24	      true
    25	    end
    26	  end
    27	
    28	  # We also clean svgs in UploadCreator#clean_svg!,
    29	  # but this is a good extra fallback.
    30	  def validate_svg(upload)
    31	    content =
    32	      begin
    33	        upload.content
    34	      rescue StandardError
    35	        nil
    36	      end
    37	    return false if content.blank?
    38	
    39	    svg = Nokogiri.XML(content).at_css("svg")
    40	    return false if svg.blank?
    41	
    42	    has_scripts = svg.xpath("//*[local-name()='script']").present?
    43	    has_event_handlers = svg.xpath("//@*[starts-with(local-name(), 'on')]").present?
    44	
    45	    !has_scripts && !has_event_handlers
    46	  end
    47	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class UploadRecovery
     4	  def initialize(dry_run: false, stop_on_error: false)
     5	    @dry_run = dry_run
     6	    @stop_on_error = stop_on_error
     7	  end
     8	
     9	  def recover(posts = Post)
    10	    posts.have_uploads.find_each { |post| recover_post post }
    11	  end
    12	
    13	  def recover_post(post)
    14	    begin
    15	      analyzer = PostAnalyzer.new(post.raw, post.topic_id)
    16	
    17	      analyzer
    18	        .cooked_stripped
    19	        .css("img", "a")
    20	        .each do |media|
    21	          if media.name == "img" && orig_src = media["data-orig-src"]
    22	            if dom_class = media["class"]
    23	              next if (Post.allowed_image_classes & dom_class.split).count > 0
    24	            end
    25	
    26	            if @dry_run
    27	              puts "#{post.full_url} #{orig_src}"
    28	            else
    29	              recover_post_upload(post, Upload.sha1_from_short_url(orig_src))
    30	            end
    31	          elsif url = (media["href"] || media["src"])
    32	            data = Upload.extract_url(url)
    33	            next unless data
    34	
    35	            upload = Upload.get_from_url(url)
    36	
    37	            if !upload || upload.verification_status == Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag]
    38	              if @dry_run
    39	                puts "#{post.full_url} #{url}"
    40	              else
    41	                sha1 = data[2]
    42	                recover_post_upload(post, sha1)
    43	              end
    44	            end
    45	          end
    46	        end
    47	    rescue => e
    48	      raise e if @stop_on_error
    49	      puts "#{post.full_url} #{e.class}: #{e.message}"
    50	    end
    51	  end
    52	
    53	  private
    54	
    55	  def recover_post_upload(post, sha1)
    56	    return unless valid_sha1?(sha1)
    57	
    58	    attributes = { post: post, sha1: sha1 }
    59	
    60	    if Discourse.store.external?
    61	      recover_post_upload_from_s3(**attributes)
    62	    else
    63	      recover_post_upload_from_local(**attributes)
    64	    end
    65	  end
    66	
    67	  def ensure_upload!(post:, sha1:, upload:)
    68	    return if !upload.persisted?
    69	
    70	    if upload.sha1 != sha1
    71	      STDERR.puts "Warning #{post.url} had an incorrect #{sha1} should be #{upload.sha1} storing in custom field 'rake uploads:fix_relative_upload_links' can fix this"
    72	
    73	      sha_map = post.custom_fields["UPLOAD_SHA1_MAP"] || "{}"
    74	      sha_map = JSON.parse(sha_map)
    75	      sha_map[sha1] = upload.sha1
    76	
    77	      post.custom_fields["UPLOAD_SHA1_MAP"] = sha_map.to_json
    78	      post.save_custom_fields
    79	    end
    80	
    81	    post.rebake!
    82	  end
    83	
    84	  def recover_post_upload_from_local(post:, sha1:)
    85	    recover_from_local(sha1: sha1, user_id: post.user_id) do |upload|
    86	      ensure_upload!(post: post, sha1: sha1, upload: upload)
    87	    end
    88	  end
    89	
    90	  def recover_post_upload_from_s3(post:, sha1:)
    91	    recover_from_s3(sha1: sha1, user_id: post.user_id) do |upload|
    92	      ensure_upload!(post: post, sha1: sha1, upload: upload)
    93	    end
    94	  end
    95	
    96	  def recover_from_local(sha1:, user_id:)
    97	    @paths ||=
    98	      begin
    99	        Dir.glob(File.join(Discourse.store.tombstone_dir, "original", "**", "*.*")).concat(
   100	          Dir.glob(File.join(Discourse.store.upload_path, "original", "**", "*.*")),
   101	        )
   102	      end
   103	
   104	    @paths.each do |path|
   105	      if path =~ /#{sha1}/
   106	        begin
   107	          tmp = Tempfile.new
   108	          tmp.write(File.read(path))
   109	          tmp.rewind
   110	
   111	          upload = create_upload(tmp, File.basename(path), user_id)
   112	          yield upload if block_given?
   113	        ensure
   114	          tmp&.close
   115	        end
   116	      end
   117	    end
   118	  end
   119	
   120	  def recover_from_s3(sha1:, user_id:)
   121	    @object_keys ||=
   122	      begin
   123	        s3_helper = Discourse.store.s3_helper
   124	
   125	        if Rails.configuration.multisite
   126	          current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
   127	          s3_helper
   128	            .list("uploads/#{current_db}/original")
   129	            .map(&:key)
   130	            .concat(
   131	              s3_helper.list(
   132	                "uploads/#{FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX}#{current_db}/original",
   133	              ).map(&:key),
   134	            )
   135	        else
   136	          s3_helper
   137	            .list("original")
   138	            .map(&:key)
   139	            .concat(s3_helper.list("#{FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX}original").map(&:key))
   140	        end
   141	      end
   142	
   143	    upload_exists = Upload.exists?(sha1: sha1)
   144	
   145	    @object_keys.each do |key|
   146	      if key =~ /#{sha1}/
   147	        tombstone_prefix = FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX
   148	
   149	        if key.include?(tombstone_prefix)
   150	          old_key = key
   151	          key = key.sub(tombstone_prefix, "")
   152	          Discourse.store.copy_file(source: old_key, destination: key, secure: false)
   153	        end
   154	
   155	        next if upload_exists
   156	
   157	        url = "https:#{SiteSetting.Upload.absolute_base_url}/#{key}"
   158	
   159	        begin
   160	          tmp =
   161	            FileHelper.download(
   162	              url,
   163	              max_file_size: SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes,
   164	              tmp_file_name: "recover_from_s3",
   165	            )
   166	
   167	          if tmp
   168	            upload = create_upload(tmp, File.basename(key), user_id)
   169	            yield upload if block_given?
   170	          end
   171	        ensure
   172	          tmp&.close
   173	        end
   174	      end
   175	    end
   176	  end
   177	
   178	  def create_upload(file, filename, user_id)
   179	    UploadCreator.new(file, filename).create_for(user_id)
   180	  end
   181	
   182	  def valid_sha1?(sha1)
   183	    sha1.present? && sha1.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
   184	  end
   185	end

     3	module Jobs

     5	    MIN_PERIOD = 30
     6	    MAX_PERIOD = 120
     7	
     8	    def execute_onceoff(args)
     9	      UploadRecovery.new.recover(Post.where("baked_at >= ?", grace_period.days.ago))
    10	    end

    14	    end
    15	  end

     3	class PostHotlinkedMedia < ActiveRecord::Base
     4	  belongs_to :post
     5	  belongs_to :upload
     6	  enum :status,
     7	       {
     8	         downloaded: "downloaded",
     9	         too_large: "too_large",
    10	         download_failed: "download_failed",
    11	         upload_create_failed: "upload_create_failed",
    12	       }
    13	
    14	  def self.normalize_src(src, reset_scheme: true)
    15	    uri = Addressable::URI.heuristic_parse(src)
    16	    uri.normalize!
    17	    uri.scheme = nil if reset_scheme
    18	    uri.to_s

     3	module Jobs
     4	  class PullHotlinkedImages < ::Jobs::Base
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: "low"
     6	
     7	    def execute(args)
     8	      disable_if_low_on_disk_space
     9	
    10	      @post_id = args[:post_id]
    11	      raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:post_id) if @post_id.blank?
    12	
    13	      # in test we have no choice cause we don't want to cause a deadlock
    14	      if Jobs.run_immediately?
    15	        pull_hotlinked_images
    16	      else
    17	        DistributedMutex.synchronize("pull_hotlinked_images_#{@post_id}", validity: 2.minutes) do
    18	          pull_hotlinked_images
    19	        end
    20	      end
    21	    end
    22	
    23	    def pull_hotlinked_images
    24	      post = Post.find_by(id: @post_id)
    25	      return if post.nil? || post.topic.nil?
    26	
    27	      hotlinked_map = post.post_hotlinked_media.index_by { |r| r.url }
    28	
    29	      changed_hotlink_records = false
    30	
    31	      extract_images_from(post.cooked).each do |node|
    32	        download_src =
    33	          original_src = node["src"] || node[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR] || node["href"]
    34	        download_src = replace_encoded_src(download_src)
    35	        download_src =
    36	          "#{SiteSetting.force_https ? "https" : "http"}:#{original_src}" if original_src.start_with?(
    37	          "//",
    38	        )
    39	        normalized_src = normalize_src(download_src)
    40	
    41	        next if !should_download_image?(download_src, post)
    42	
    43	        hotlink_record = hotlinked_map[normalized_src]
    44	
    45	        if hotlink_record.nil?
    46	          hotlinked_map[normalized_src] = hotlink_record =
    47	            PostHotlinkedMedia.new(post: post, url: normalized_src)
    48	          begin
    49	            hotlink_record.upload = attempt_download(download_src, post.user_id)
    50	            hotlink_record.status = :downloaded
    51	          rescue ImageTooLargeError
    52	            hotlink_record.status = :too_large
    53	          rescue ImageBrokenError
    54	            hotlink_record.status = :download_failed
    55	          rescue UploadCreateError
    56	            hotlink_record.status = :upload_create_failed
    57	          end
    58	        end
    59	
    60	        if hotlink_record.changed?
    61	          changed_hotlink_records = true
    62	          hotlink_record.save!
    63	        end
    64	      rescue => e
    65	        raise e if Rails.env.test?
    66	        log(
    67	          :error,
    68	          "Failed to pull hotlinked image (#{download_src}) post: #{@post_id}\n" + e.message +
    69	            "\n" + e.backtrace.join("\n"),
    70	        )
    71	      end
    72	
    73	      if changed_hotlink_records
    74	        post.trigger_post_process(
    75	          bypass_bump: true,
    76	          skip_pull_hotlinked_images: true, # Avoid an infinite loop of job scheduling
    77	        )
    78	      end
    79	
    80	      if hotlinked_map.size > 0
    81	        Jobs.cancel_scheduled_job(:update_hotlinked_raw, post_id: post.id)
    82	        update_raw_delay = SiteSetting.editing_grace_period + 1
    83	        Jobs.enqueue_in(update_raw_delay, :update_hotlinked_raw, post_id: post.id)
    84	      end
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    def download(src)
    88	      downloaded = nil
    89	
    90	      begin
    91	        retries ||= 3
    92	
    93	        if SiteSetting.verbose_upload_logging
    94	          Rails.logger.warn("Verbose Upload Logging: Downloading hotlinked image from #{src}")
    95	        end
    96	
    97	        downloaded =
    98	          FileHelper.download(
    99	            src,
   100	            max_file_size: SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes,
   101	            retain_on_max_file_size_exceeded: true,
   102	            tmp_file_name: "discourse-hotlinked",
   103	            follow_redirect: true,
   104	            read_timeout: 15,
   105	          )
   106	      rescue => e
   107	        if SiteSetting.verbose_upload_logging
   108	          Rails.logger.warn("Verbose Upload Logging: Error '#{e.message}' while downloading #{src}")
   109	        end
   110	
   111	        if (retries -= 1) > 0 && !Rails.env.test?
   112	          sleep 1
   113	          retry
   114	        end
   115	      end
   116	
   117	      downloaded
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    class ImageTooLargeError < StandardError

     4	  class PullUserProfileHotlinkedImages < ::Jobs::PullHotlinkedImages
     5	    def execute(args)
     6	      @user_id = args[:user_id]
     7	      raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:user_id) if @user_id.blank?
     8	
     9	      user_profile = UserProfile.find_by(user_id: @user_id)
    10	      return if user_profile.blank? || user_profile.bio_cooked.nil?
    11

    13	      broken_image_urls = []
    14	      downloaded_images = {}

    17	        download_src = original_src = node["src"] || node["href"]
    18	        download_src =

    20	          "//",
    21	        )
    22	        normalized_src = normalize_src(download_src)
    23	
    24	        next if !should_download_image?(download_src)
    25	
    26	        begin
    27	          already_attempted_download =
    28	            downloaded_images.include?(normalized_src) ||
    29	              large_image_urls.include?(normalized_src) ||
    30	              broken_image_urls.include?(normalized_src)
    31	          if !already_attempted_download
    32	            downloaded_images[normalized_src] = attempt_download(download_src, @user_id)
    33	          end
    34	        rescue ImageTooLargeError
    35	          large_image_urls << normalized_src
    36	        rescue ImageBrokenError
    37	          broken_image_urls << normalized_src
    38	        end
    39	      rescue => e
    40	        raise e if Rails.env.test?
    41	        log(
    42	          :error,
    43	          "Failed to pull hotlinked image (#{download_src}) user: #{@user_id}\n" + e.message +
    44	            "\n" + e.backtrace.join("\n"),
    45	        )
    46	      end
    47	
    48	      user_profile.bio_raw =
    49	        InlineUploads.replace_hotlinked_image_urls(raw: user_profile.bio_raw) do |match_src|
    50	          normalized_match_src = PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(match_src)
    51	          downloaded_images[normalized_match_src]
    52	        end
    53	
    54	      user_profile.skip_pull_hotlinked_image = true
    55	      user_profile.save!
    56	    end
    57	  end
    58	end

     3	class Admin::EmojiController < Admin::AdminController
     4	  def index
     5	    render_serialized(Emoji.custom, EmojiSerializer, root: false)
     6	  end
     7	
     8	  # NOTE: This kind of custom logic also needs to be implemented to
     9	  # be run in the ExternalUploadManager when a direct S3 upload is completed,
    10	  # related to preventDirectS3Uploads in the UppyUploadMixin.
    11	  #
    12	  # Until then, preventDirectS3Uploads is set to true in the UppyUploadMixin.
    13	  def create
    14	    file = params[:file] || params[:files].first
    15	    name = params[:name] || File.basename(file.original_filename, ".*")
    16	    group = params[:group] ? params[:group].downcase : nil
    17	
    18	    hijack do
    19	      # fix the name
    20	      name = File.basename(name, ".*")
    21	      name = Emoji.sanitize_emoji_name(name)
    22	      upload =
    23	        UploadCreator.new(file.tempfile, file.original_filename, type: "custom_emoji").create_for(
    24	          current_user.id,
    25	        )
    26	
    27	      good = true
    28	
    29	      data =
    30	        if upload.persisted?
    31	          custom_emoji =
    32	            CustomEmoji.new(name: name, upload: upload, group: group, user: current_user)
    33	
    34	          if custom_emoji.save
    35	            StaffActionLogger.new(current_user).log_custom_emoji_create(name, group: group)
    36	
    37	            Emoji.clear_cache
    38	            { name: custom_emoji.name, url: custom_emoji.upload.url, group: group }
    39	          else
    40	            good = false
    41	            failed_json.merge(errors: custom_emoji.errors.full_messages)
    42	          end
    43	        else
    44	          good = false
    45	          failed_json.merge(errors: upload.errors.full_messages)
    46	        end
    47	
    48	      render json: data.as_json, status: good ? 200 : 422
    49	    end
    50	  end
    51	
    52	  def destroy
    53	    name = params.require(:id)
    54	
    55	    # NOTE: the upload will automatically be removed by the 'clean_up_uploads' job
    56	    emoji = CustomEmoji.find_by(name: name)
    57	
    58	    if emoji.present?
    59	      StaffActionLogger.new(current_user).log_custom_emoji_destroy(name)
    60	      emoji.destroy!
    61	    end
    62	
    63	    Emoji.clear_cache
    64	
    65	    Jobs.enqueue(:rebake_custom_emoji_posts, name: name)
    66	
    67	    render json: success_json
    68	  end

The storage side has a strong invariant: `Upload.url` is both a database contract and a physical object locator. S3 inventory, restore remaps, email attachment reads, thumbnail generation, and secure-upload redirects all derive paths from it, sometimes by regex rather than associations.

   128	
   129	    def attempt_download(src, user_id)
   130	      # secure-uploads endpoint prevents anonymous downloads, so we
   131	      # need the presigned S3 URL here
   132	      if Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src)
   133	        src = Upload.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url(src, include_content_disposition: false)
   134	      end
   135	
   136	      hotlinked = download(src)
   137	      raise ImageBrokenError if !hotlinked
   138	      if File.size(hotlinked.path) > SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes
   139	        raise ImageTooLargeError
   140	      end
   141	
   142	      filename = File.basename(URI.parse(src).path)
   143	      filename << File.extname(hotlinked.path) unless filename["."]
   144	      upload = UploadCreator.new(hotlinked, filename, origin: src).create_for(user_id)
   145	
   146	      if upload.persisted?
   147	        upload
   148	      else
   149	        log(
   150	          :info,
   151	          "Failed to persist downloaded hotlinked image for post: #{@post_id}: #{src} - #{upload.errors.full_messages.join("\n")}",
   152	        )
   153	        raise UploadCreateError
   154	      end
   155	    end

   158	      doc = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(html)
   159	
   160	      doc.css("img[src], [#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}], a.lightbox[href]") -
   161	        doc.css("img.avatar") - doc.css(".lightbox img[src]")
   162	    end

   164	    def should_download_image?(src, post = nil)
   165	      # make sure we actually have a url
   166	      return false if src.blank?
   167	
   168	      local_bases =
   169	        [Discourse.base_url, Discourse.asset_host, SiteSetting.external_emoji_url.presence].compact
   170	          .map { |s| normalize_src(s) }
   171	
   172	      if Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(src) || normalize_src(src).start_with?(*local_bases) ||
   173	           src =~ %r{\A/[^/]}i
   174	        return false if !(src =~ %r{/uploads/} || Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src))
   175	
   176	        # Someone could hotlink a file from a different site on the same CDN,
   177	        # so check whether we have it in this database
   178	        #
   179	        # if the upload already exists and is attached to a different post,
   180	        # or the original_sha1 is missing meaning it was created before secure
   181	        # media was enabled, then we definitely want to redownload again otherwise
   182	        # we end up reusing existing uploads which may be linked to many posts
   183	        # already.
   184	        upload = Upload.consider_for_reuse(Upload.get_from_url(src), post)
   185	
   186	        return !upload.present?
   187	      end
   188	
   189	      # Don't download non-local images unless site setting enabled
   190	      return false unless SiteSetting.download_remote_images_to_local?
   191	
   192	      # parse the src
   193	      begin
   194	        uri = URI.parse(src)
   195	      rescue URI::Error
   196	        return false
   197	      end
   198	
   199	      hostname = uri.hostname
   200	      return false unless hostname
   201	
   202	      # check the domains blocklist
   203	      SiteSetting.should_download_images?(src)
   204	    end

    11	      post = Post.find_by(id: @post_id)

    20	          normalized_match_src = PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(match_src)
    21	          hotlinked_map[normalized_match_src]&.upload
    22	        end
    23	
    24	      if post.raw != raw
    25	        changes = { raw: raw, edit_reason: I18n.t("upload.edit_reason") }

    28	          changes,
    29	          bypass_bump: true,
    30	          skip_staff_log: true,
    31	          skip_validations: true,
    32	        )
    33	      end
    34	    end

     3	module DiscourseAi
     4	  module Completions
     5	    class UploadEncoder
     6	      def self.encode(
     7	        upload_ids:,
     8	        max_pixels:,
     9	        allowed_kinds: [:image],
    10	        allowed_attachment_types: nil
    11	      )
    12	        uploads = []
    13	        allowed_attachment_types = normalize_attachment_types(allowed_attachment_types)
    14	        upload_ids.each do |upload_id|
    15	          upload = Upload.find(upload_id)
    16	          next if upload.blank?
    17	
    18	          extension = upload.extension&.downcase
    19	          kind = image_extension?(extension) ? :image : :document
    20	
    21	          next if allowed_kinds.exclude?(kind)
    22	
    23	          if kind == :document
    24	            mime_type =
    25	              MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(upload.original_filename)&.content_type ||
    26	                "application/octet-stream"
    27	
    28	            attachment_type = attachment_type_for(upload.extension, mime_type)
    29	            next if disallowed_attachment?(allowed_attachment_types, attachment_type)
    30	
    31	            payload = encode_document(upload, mime_type)
    32	            uploads << payload if payload
    33	            next
    34	          end
    35	
    36	          next if upload.width.to_i == 0 || upload.height.to_i == 0
    37	
    38	          desired_extension = upload.extension
    39	          desired_extension = "png" if upload.extension == "gif"
    40	          desired_extension = "png" if upload.extension == "webp"
    41	          desired_extension = "jpeg" if upload.extension == "jpg"
    42	
    43	          # this keeps it very simple format wise given everyone supports png and jpg
    44	          next if !%w[jpeg png].include?(desired_extension)
    45	
    46	          payload = encode_image(upload, desired_extension, max_pixels)
    47	          uploads << payload if payload
    48	        end
    49	        uploads
    50	      end
    51	
    52	      def self.attachment_type_for(extension, mime_type)
    53	        ext = extension.to_s.delete_prefix(".").downcase
    54	        mime = mime_type.to_s
    55	
    56	        return "pdf" if ext == "pdf" || mime.include?("pdf")
    57	        return "docx" if ext == "docx"
    58	        return "doc" if ext == "doc"
    59	        return "txt" if ext == "txt" || mime.include?("text/plain")
    60	        return "rtf" if ext == "rtf"
    61	        return "html" if %w[html htm].include?(ext) || mime.include?("html")
    62	        return "markdown" if %w[md markdown].include?(ext) || mime.include?("markdown")
    63	
    64	        "file"
    65	      end
    66	
    67	      class << self
    68	        private
    69	
    70	        def normalize_attachment_types(types)
    71	          return nil if types.nil?
    72	
    73	          Array(types).map(&:downcase)
    74	        end
    75	
    76	        def disallowed_attachment?(allowed_types, attachment_type)
    77	          !allowed_types.nil? && !allowed_types.include?(attachment_type)
    78	        end
    79	
    80	        def image_extension?(ext)
    81	          %w[jpg jpeg png gif webp].include?(ext)
    82	        end
    83	
    84	        def encode_document(upload, mime_type)
    85	          path = fetch_path(upload)
    86	          return if path.blank?
    87	
    88	          encoded = Base64.strict_encode64(File.binread(path))
    89	
    90	          {
    91	            base64: encoded,
    92	            mime_type: mime_type,
    93	            kind: :document,
    94	            filename: upload.original_filename,
    95	          }
    96	        end
    97	
    98	        def encode_image(upload, desired_extension, max_pixels)
    99	          original_pixels = upload.width * upload.height
   100	
   101	          image = upload
   102	
   103	          if original_pixels > max_pixels
   104	            ratio = max_pixels.to_f / original_pixels
   105	
   106	            new_width = (ratio * upload.width).to_i
   107	            new_height = (ratio * upload.height).to_i
   108	
   109	            image = upload.get_optimized_image(new_width, new_height, format: desired_extension)
   110	          elsif upload.extension != desired_extension
   111	            image =
   112	              upload.get_optimized_image(upload.width, upload.height, format: desired_extension)
   113	          end
   114	
   115	          return if !image
   116	
   117	          mime_type = MiniMime.lookup_by_filename("test.#{desired_extension}").content_type
   118	
   119	          path = fetch_path(image)
   120	          return if path.blank?

     3	module Jobs
     4	  class DigestRagUpload < ::Jobs::Base
     5	    CHUNK_SIZE = 1024
     6	    CHUNK_OVERLAP = 64
     7	    MAX_FRAGMENTS = 100_000
     8	
     9	    # TODO(roman): Add a way to automatically recover from errors, resulting in unindexed uploads.
    10	    def execute(args)
    11	      return unless upload = Upload.find_by(id: args[:upload_id])
    12	      return unless target_type = args[:target_type]
    13	      return unless target_id = args[:target_id]
    14	      return unless target = target_type.constantize.find_by(id: target_id)
    15	
    16	      vector_rep = DiscourseAi::Embeddings::Vector.instance
    17	
    18	      tokenizer = vector_rep.tokenizer
    19	      chunk_tokens = target.rag_chunk_tokens
    20	      overlap_tokens = target.rag_chunk_overlap_tokens
    21	
    22	      fragment_ids = RagDocumentFragment.where(target:, upload:).pluck(:id)
    23	
    24	      # Check if this is the first time we process this upload.
    25	      if fragment_ids.empty?
    26	        document = get_uploaded_file(upload:, target:)
    27	        return if document.nil?
    28	
    29	        RagDocumentFragment.publish_status(upload, { total: 0, indexed: 0, left: 0 })
    30	
    31	        fragment_ids = []
    32	        idx = 0
    33	
    34	        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
    35	          chunk_document(
    36	            file: document,
    37	            tokenizer:,
    38	            chunk_tokens:,
    39	            overlap_tokens:,
    40	          ) do |chunk, metadata|
    41	            fragment_ids << RagDocumentFragment.create!(
    42	              target:,
    43	              fragment: chunk,
    44	              fragment_number: idx + 1,
    45	              upload:,
    46	              metadata:,
    47	            ).id
    48	
    49	            idx += 1
    50	
    51	            if idx > MAX_FRAGMENTS
    52	              Rails.logger.warn("Upload #{upload.id} has too many fragments, truncating.")
    53	              break
    54	            end
    55	          end
    56	        end
    57	      end
    58	
    59	      fragment_ids.each_slice(50) do |slice|
    60	        Jobs.enqueue(:generate_rag_embeddings, fragment_ids: slice)
    61	      end
    62	    ensure
    63	      @file&.close
    64	    end
    65	
    66	    private
    67	
    68	    def chunk_document(file:, tokenizer:, chunk_tokens:, overlap_tokens:)
    69	      buffer = +""
    70	      current_metadata = nil
    71	      done = false
    72	      overlap = ""
    73	
    74	      # generally this will be plenty
    75	      read_size = chunk_tokens * 10
    76	
    77	      while buffer.present? || !done
    78	        if buffer.length < read_size
    79	          read = file.read(read_size)
    80	          done = true if read.nil?
    81	
    82	          read = Encodings.to_utf8(read) if read
    83	
    84	          buffer << (read || "")
    85	        end
    86	
    87	        # at this point we unconditionally have 2x CHUNK_SIZE worth of data in the buffer
    88	        metadata_regex = /\[\[metadata (.*?)\]\]/m
    89	
    90	        before_metadata, new_metadata, after_metadata = buffer.split(metadata_regex)
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Jobs
     4	  class SharedConversationAdjustUploadSecurity < ::Jobs::Base
     5	    def execute(args)
     6	      if args[:conversation_id].present?
     7	        # The conversation context includes post cooked content so this
     8	        # must be updated when target uploads security changes.
     9	        update_conversation(args[:conversation_id])
    10	      elsif args[:target_id].present? && args[:target_type].present?
    11	        # If we deleted the conversation then we just need to update the target's
    12	        # uploads security, no need to update the conversation.
    13	        update_target(args[:target_id], args[:target_type])
    14	      end
    15	    end
    16	
    17	    private
    18	
    19	    def update_conversation(conversation_id)
    20	      conversation = SharedAiConversation.find_by(id: conversation_id)
    21	      return if conversation.blank?
    22	
    23	      # NOTE: Only Topics are supported for now, in future we will need a more flexible
    24	      # way of doing this.
    25	      if conversation.target_type == "Topic"
    26	        rebaked_posts = TopicUploadSecurityManager.new(conversation.target).run
    27	
    28	        if rebaked_posts.any?
    29	          new_context =
    30	            conversation.context.map do |context_post|
    31	              rebaked_post = rebaked_posts.find { |p| p.id == context_post["id"] }
    32	              context_post["cooked"] = rebaked_post.cooked if rebaked_post
    33	              context_post
    34	            end
    35	
    36	          conversation.update(context: new_context)
    37	        end
    38	      end
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    def update_target(target_id, target_type)
    42	      # NOTE: Only Topics are supported for now, in future we will need a more flexible
    43	      # way of doing this.
    44	      if target_type == "Topic"
    45	        topic = target_type.constantize.find_by(id: target_id)
    46	        return if topic.blank?
    47	        TopicUploadSecurityManager.new(topic).run
    48	      end
    49	    end
    50	  end
    51	end

   674	    all_upload_ids_changed, post_ids_to_rebake = nil
